name: AdwAboutDialog
doc: A dialog showing information about the application. an about dialog is typically opened when the user activates the `About …` item in the application's primary menu.

properties.name: application-icon
properties.doc: The name of the application icon.

properties.name: application-name
properties.doc: The name of the application.

properties.name: artists
properties.doc: The list of artists of the application.

properties.name: comments
properties.doc:
The comments about the application.

Comments will be shown on the Details page, above links.

Unlike [property@Gtk.AboutDialog:comments], this string can be long and
detailed.

properties.name: copyright
properties.doc: The copyright information.

properties.name: debug-info
properties.doc:
The debug information.

Debug information will be shown on the Troubleshooting page.

properties.name: debug-info-filename
properties.doc: The debug information filename.

properties.name: designers
properties.doc: The list of designers of the application.

properties.name: developer-name
properties.doc: The developer name.

properties.name: developers
properties.doc: The list of developers of the application.

properties.name: documenters
properties.doc: The list of documenters of the application.

properties.name: issue-url
properties.doc: The URL for the application's issue tracker.

properties.name: license
properties.doc: The license text.

properties.name: license-type
properties.doc:
The license type.

Allows to set the application's license froma list of known licenses.

If the application's license is not in the list,
[property@AboutDialog:license] can be used instead.

properties.name: release-notes
properties.doc:
The release notes of the application. Release notes are displayed on the the What's New page. Release notes are formatted the same way as [AppStream descriptions](https://freedesktop.org/software/appstream/docs/chap-Metadata.html#tag-description). The supported formatting options are: * Paragraph (`

`) * Ordered list (`

    `), with list items (`
  1. `) * Unordered list (`
      `), with list items (`
    • `) Within paragraphs and list items, emphasis (``) and inline code (``) text styles are supported.

properties.name: release-notes-version
properties.doc: The version described by the application's release notes.

properties.name: support-url
properties.doc: The URL of the application's support page.

properties.name: translator-credits
properties.doc: The translator credits string.

properties.name: version
properties.doc: The version of the application.

properties.name: website
properties.doc: The URL of the application's website.

properties.name: can-close
properties.doc: Whether the dialog can be closed.

properties.name: child
properties.doc: The child widget of the `AdwDialog`.

properties.name: content-height
properties.doc: The height of the dialog's contents.

properties.name: content-width
properties.doc: The width of the dialog's contents.

properties.name: current-breakpoint
properties.doc: The current breakpoint.

properties.name: default-widget
properties.doc: The default widget.

properties.name: focus-widget
properties.doc: The focus widget.

properties.name: follows-content-size
properties.doc:
Whether to size content automatically.

If set to `TRUE`, always use the content's natural size instead of
[property@Dialog:content-width] and [property@Dialog:content-height].

properties.name: presentation-mode
properties.doc: The dialog's presentation mode.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: The title of the dialog.

properties.name: can-focus
properties.doc:
Whether the widget or any of its descendents can accept
the input focus.

properties.name: can-target
properties.doc: Whether the widget can receive pointer events.

properties.name: css-classes
properties.doc: A list of css classes applied to this widget.

properties.name: css-name
properties.doc: The name of this widget in the CSS tree.

properties.name: cursor
properties.doc: The cursor used by @widget.

properties.name: focus-on-click
properties.doc: Whether the widget should grab focus when it is clicked with the mouse.

properties.name: focusable
properties.doc: Whether this widget itself will accept the input focus.

properties.name: halign
properties.doc: How to distribute horizontal space if widget gets extra space.

properties.name: has-default
properties.doc: Whether the widget is the default widget.

properties.name: has-focus
properties.doc: Whether the widget has the input focus.

properties.name: has-tooltip
properties.doc:
Enables or disables the emission of the [signal@Gtk.Widget::query-tooltip]
signal on @widget.

properties.name: height-request
properties.doc: Overrides for height request of the widget.

properties.name: hexpand
properties.doc: Whether to expand horizontally.

properties.name: hexpand-set
properties.doc: Whether to use the `hexpand` property.

properties.name: layout-manager
properties.doc:
The [class@Gtk.LayoutManager] instance to use to compute
the preferred size of the widget, and allocate its children.

properties.name: limit-events
properties.doc:
Makes this widget act like a modal dialog, with respect to
event delivery.

Global event controllers will not handle events with targets
inside the widget, unless they are set up to ignore propagation
limits.

properties.name: margin-bottom
properties.doc: Margin on bottom side of widget.

properties.name: margin-end
properties.doc: Margin on end of widget, horizontally.

properties.name: margin-start
properties.doc: Margin on start of widget, horizontally.

properties.name: margin-top
properties.doc: Margin on top side of widget.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the widget.

properties.name: opacity
properties.doc: The requested opacity of the widget.

properties.name: overflow
properties.doc: How content outside the widget's content area is treated.

properties.name: parent
properties.doc: The parent widget of this widget.

properties.name: receives-default
properties.doc: Whether the widget will receive the default action when it is focused.

properties.name: root
properties.doc: The `GtkRoot` widget of the widget tree containing this widget.

properties.name: scale-factor
properties.doc: The scale factor of the widget.

properties.name: sensitive
properties.doc: Whether the widget responds to input.

properties.name: tooltip-markup
properties.doc:
Sets the text of tooltip to be the given string, which is marked up
with Pango markup.

properties.name: tooltip-text
properties.doc: Sets the text of tooltip to be the given string.

properties.name: valign
properties.doc: How to distribute vertical space if widget gets extra space.

properties.name: vexpand
properties.doc: Whether to expand vertically.

properties.name: vexpand-set
properties.doc: Whether to use the `vexpand` property.

properties.name: visible
properties.doc: Whether the widget is visible.

properties.name: width-request
properties.doc: Overrides for width request of the widget.

signals.name: activate-link
signals.doc: Emitted when a URL is activated.

signals.name: close-attempt
signals.doc:
Emitted when the close button or shortcut is used, or
[method@Dialog.close] is called while [property@Dialog:can-close] is set to
`FALSE`.

signals.name: closed
signals.doc: Emitted when the dialog is successfully closed.

signals.name: destroy
signals.doc:
Signals that all holders of a reference to the widget should release
the reference that they hold.

signals.name: direction-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the text direction of a widget changes.

signals.name: hide
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is hidden.

signals.name: keynav-failed
signals.doc: Emitted if keyboard navigation fails.

signals.name: map
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is going to be mapped.

signals.name: mnemonic-activate
signals.doc: Emitted when a widget is activated via a mnemonic.

signals.name: move-focus
signals.doc: Emitted when the focus is moved.

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

signals.name: realize
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is associated with a `GdkSurface`.

signals.name: show
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is shown.

signals.name: state-flags-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the widget state changes.

signals.name: unmap
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is going to be unmapped.

signals.name: unrealize
signals.doc: Emitted when the `GdkSurface` associated with @widget is destroyed.

name: AdwAlertDialog

`FALSE`.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

name: AdwAnimation
doc:
A base class for animations.

`AdwAnimation` represents an animation on a widget.

properties.name: follow-enable-animations-setting
properties.doc:
Whether to skip the animation when animations are globally disabled.

The default behavior is to skip the animation.

properties.name: state
properties.doc: The animation state.

properties.name: target
properties.doc: The target to animate.

properties.name: value
properties.doc: The current value of the animation.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc:
The animation widget.

It provides the frame clock for the animation.

signals.name: done
signals.doc:
This signal is emitted when the animation has been completed, either on its
own or via calling [method@Animation.skip].

name: AdwAnimationTarget
doc: Represents a value [class@Animation] can animate.

name: AdwApplication
doc:
A base class for Adwaita applications.

`AdwApplication` handles library initialization by calling [func@init] in the
default [signal@Gio.Application::startup] signal handler, in turn chaining up
as required by [class@Gtk.Application].

properties.name: style-manager
properties.doc: The style manager for this application.

properties.name: active-window
properties.doc: The currently focused window of the application.

properties.name: menubar
properties.doc: The menu model to be used for the application's menu bar.

properties.name: register-session
properties.doc: Set this property to true to register with the session manager.

properties.name: screensaver-active
properties.doc:
This property is true if GTK believes that the screensaver
is currently active.

signals.name: query-end
signals.doc: Emitted when the session manager is about to end the session.

signals.name: window-added
signals.doc: Emitted when a window is added to an application.

signals.name: window-removed
signals.doc: Emitted when a window is removed from an application.

name: AdwApplicationWindow
doc: A freeform application window. `AdwApplicationWindow` is a [class@Gtk.ApplicationWindow] subclass providing the same features as [class@Window]. See [class@Window] for details. Example of an `AdwApplicationWindow` UI definition: ```xml

properties.name: adaptive-preview
properties.doc:
Whether adaptive preview is currently open.

Adaptive preview is a debugging tool used for testing the window
contents at specific screen sizes, simulating mobile environment.

Adaptive preview can always be accessed from inspector.

doc: A bottom sheet with an optional bottom bar. `AdwBottomSheet` has three child widgets.

properties.doc:
Horizontal alignment of the bottom sheet.

0 means the bottom sheet is flush with the start edge, 1 means it's flush
with the end edge.

name: AdwBreakpoint
doc:
Describes a breakpoint for [class@Window] or [class@Dialog].

Breakpoints are used to create adaptive UI, allowing to change the layout
depending on available size.

Breakpoint is a size threshold, specified by its condition, as well as one or
more setters.

Each setter has a target object, a property and a value.

properties.name: condition
properties.doc: The breakpoint's condition.

signals.name: apply
signals.doc: Emitted when the breakpoint is applied.

signals.name: unapply
signals.doc: Emitted when the breakpoint is unapplied.

name: AdwBreakpointBin

name: AdwButtonContent
doc: A helper widget for creating buttons. `AdwButtonContent` is a box-like widget with an icon and a label. It's intended to be used as a direct child of [class@Gtk.Button], [class@Gtk.MenuButton] or [class@SplitButton], when they need to have both an icon and a label, as follows: ```xml ``` `AdwButtonContent` handles style classes and connecting the mnemonic to the button automatically. ## CSS nodes ``` buttoncontent ╰── box ├── image ╰── label ``` `AdwButtonContent`'s CSS node is called `buttoncontent`.

name: AdwButtonRow
doc: A [class@Gtk.ListBoxRow] that looks like a button. The `AdwButtonRow` widget has a title and two icons: before and after the title. It is convenient for presenting actions like "Delete" at the end of a boxed list. `AdwButtonRow` is always activatable. ## CSS nodes `AdwButtonRow` has a main CSS node with name `row` and the style class `.button`. It contains the subnode `box` for its main horizontal box, which contains the nodes: `image.icon.start` for the start icon, `label.title` for the title, and `image.icon.end` for the end icon. ## Style classes The [`.suggested-action`](style-classes.html#suggested-action) style class makes `AdwButtonRow` use accent color for its background.

properties.name: end-icon-name

name: AdwClamp
doc: A widget constraining its child to a given size. The `AdwClamp` widget constrains the size of the widget it contains to a given maximum size.

properties.name: child

properties.name: tightening-threshold
properties.doc:
The size above which the child is clamped.

Starting from this size, the clamp will tighten its grip on the child,
slowly allocating less and less of the available size up to the maximum
allocated size.

name: AdwClampLayout
doc: A layout manager constraining its children to a given size. `AdwClampLayout` constraints the size of the widgets it contains to a given maximum size.

properties.name: maximum-size
properties.doc: The maximum size to allocate to the children.

properties.name: tightening-threshold
properties.doc:
The size above which the children are clamped.

Starting from this size, the layout will tighten its grip on the children,
slowly allocating less and less of the available size up to the maximum
allocated size.

properties.name: unit
properties.doc: The length unit for maximum size and tightening threshold.

emit the [signal@EntryRow::apply] signal.

This is useful if changing the entry contents can trigger an expensive
operation, e.g.

name: AdwFlap
doc: An adaptive container acting like a box or an overlay. The `AdwFlap` widget can display its children like a [class@Gtk.Box] does or like a [class@Gtk.Overlay] does, according to the [property@Flap:fold-policy] value. `AdwFlap` has at most three children: [property@Flap:content], [property@Flap:flap] and [property@Flap:separator].

properties.name: content
properties.doc: The content widget.

properties.name: flap
properties.doc: The flap widget.

properties.name: flap-position
properties.doc: The flap position.

properties.name: fold-duration
properties.doc: The fold transition animation duration, in milliseconds.

properties.name: fold-policy
properties.doc: The fold policy for the flap.

properties.name: fold-threshold-policy
properties.doc:
Determines when the flap will fold.

If set to `ADW_FOLD_THRESHOLD_POLICY_MINIMUM`, flap will only fold when
the children cannot fit anymore.

properties.name: folded
properties.doc: Whether the flap is currently folded.

properties.name: locked
properties.doc:
Whether the flap is locked.

If `FALSE`, folding when the flap is revealed automatically closes it, and
unfolding it when the flap is not revealed opens it.

properties.name: modal
properties.doc:
Whether the flap is modal.

If `TRUE`, clicking the content widget while flap is revealed, as well as
pressing the <kbd>Esc</kbd> key, will close the flap.

properties.name: reveal-flap
properties.doc: Whether the flap widget is revealed.

properties.name: reveal-params
properties.doc: The reveal animation spring parameters.

properties.name: reveal-progress
properties.doc: The current reveal transition progress.

properties.name: separator
properties.doc: The separator widget.

properties.name: swipe-to-close
properties.doc: Whether the flap can be closed with a swipe gesture.

properties.name: swipe-to-open
properties.doc: Whether the flap can be opened with a swipe gesture.

properties.name: transition-type
properties.doc: the type of animation used for reveal and fold transitions.

the input focus.

If this property is not set, the

The format of the string is button names, separated by commas.

name: AdwLeaflet
doc: An adaptive container acting like a box or a stack. The `AdwLeaflet` widget can display its children like a [class@Gtk.Box] does or like a [class@Gtk.Stack] does, adapting to size changes by switching between the two modes. When there is enough space the children are displayed side by side, otherwise only one is displayed and the leaflet is said to be “folded”. The threshold is dictated by the preferred minimum sizes of the children. When a leaflet is folded, the children can be navigated using swipe gestures. The “over” and “under” transition types stack the children one on top of the other, while the “slide” transition puts the children side by side.

properties.name: can-navigate-back
properties.doc: Whether gestures and shortcuts for navigating backward are enabled.

properties.name: can-navigate-forward
properties.doc: Whether gestures and shortcuts for navigating forward are enabled.

properties.name: can-unfold
properties.doc: Whether or not the leaflet can unfold.

properties.name: child-transition-params
properties.doc: The child transition spring parameters.

properties.name: child-transition-running
properties.doc: Whether a child transition is currently running.

properties.name: fold-threshold-policy
properties.doc:
Determines when the leaflet will fold.

If set to `ADW_FOLD_THRESHOLD_POLICY_MINIMUM`, it will only fold when the
children cannot fit anymore.

properties.name: folded
properties.doc: Whether the leaflet is folded.

properties.name: homogeneous
properties.doc: Whether the leaflet allocates the same size for all children when folded.

properties.name: mode-transition-duration
properties.doc: The mode transition animation duration, in milliseconds.

properties.name: pages
properties.doc:
A selection model with the leaflet's pages.

This can be used to keep an up-to-date view.

properties.name: transition-type
properties.doc: The type of animation used for transitions between modes and children.

properties.name: visible-child
properties.doc:
The widget currently visible when the leaflet is folded.

The transition is determined by [property@Leaflet:transition-type] and
[property@Leaflet:child-transition-params].

properties.name: visible-child-name
properties.doc: The name of the widget currently visible when the leaflet is folded.

the input focus.

doc: A dialog presenting a message or a question. Message dialogs have a heading, a body, an optional child widget, and one or multiple responses, each presented as a button. Each response has a unique string ID, and a button label.

name: AdwNavigationPage
doc:
A page within [class@NavigationView] or [class@NavigationSplitView].

Each page has a child widget, a title and optionally a tag.

The [signal@NavigationPage::showing], [signal@NavigationPage::shown],
[signal@NavigationPage::hiding] and [signal@NavigationPage::hidden] signals
can be used to track the page's visibility within its `AdwNavigationView`.

## Header Bar Integration

When placed inside `AdwNavigationPage`, [class@HeaderBar] will display the
page title instead of window title.

When used together with [class@NavigationView], it will also display a back
button that can be used to go back to the previous page.

name: AdwNavigationView
doc: A page-based navigation container. `AdwNavigationView` presents one child at a time, similar to [class@Gtk.Stack]. `AdwNavigationView` can only contain [class@NavigationPage] children. It maintains a navigation stack that can be controlled with [method@NavigationView.push] and [method@NavigationView.pop].

signals.name: get-next-page
signals.doc:
Emitted when a push shortcut or a gesture is triggered.

To support the push shortcuts and gestures, the application is expected to
return the page to push in the handler.

This signal can be emitted multiple times for the gestures, for example
when the gesture is cancelled by the user.

name: AdwOverlaySplitView
doc: A widget presenting sidebar and content side by side or as an overlay. `AdwOverlaySplitView` has two children: sidebar and content, and displays them side by side. When [property@OverlaySplitView:collapsed] is set to `TRUE`, the sidebar is instead shown as an overlay above the content widget. The sidebar can be hidden or shown using the [property@OverlaySplitView:show-sidebar] property. Sidebar can be displayed before or after the content, this can be controlled with the [property@OverlaySplitView:sidebar-position] property. Collapsing the split view automatically hides the sidebar widget, and uncollapsing it shows the sidebar.

properties.name: collapsed
properties.doc: Whether the split view is collapsed.

properties.name: content
properties.doc: The content widget.

properties.doc: The maximum sidebar width.

properties.name: min-sidebar-width
properties.doc: The minimum sidebar width.

properties.name: pin-sidebar
properties.doc:
Whether the sidebar widget is pinned.

By default, collapsing @self automatically hides the sidebar widget, and
uncollapsing it shows the sidebar.

properties.name: show-sidebar
properties.doc: Whether the sidebar widget is shown.

properties.name: sidebar
properties.doc: The sidebar widget.

properties.name: sidebar-position
properties.doc: The sidebar position.

properties.name: sidebar-width-fraction
properties.doc: The preferred sidebar width as a fraction of the total width.

Adaptive preview is a debugging tool used for testing the window

Adaptive preview can always be accessed from inspector.

name: AdwShortcutsItem
doc:
An object representing an individual shortcut in [class@ShortcutsSection].

A shortcut has a title, an optional subtitle, and an accelerator.

Accelerator must be specified in the format [class@ShortcutLabel] accepts.

Alternatively, the [property@ShortcutsItem:action-name] property can be used
to automatically get accelerator associated with the specified action, as set
via [method@Gtk.Application.set_accels_for_action].

If both are specified, the accelerator will be used if the action couldn't
be found or doesn't have an accelerator associated for it.

If [property@ShortcutsItem:direction] is set, the shortcut will only be
displayed for the specified text direction.

properties.name: accelerator
properties.doc: The shortcut accelerator.

properties.name: action-name
properties.doc: Fully qualified action name to get the accelerator from.

properties.name: direction
properties.doc: The shortcut direction.

properties.name: subtitle
properties.doc: The subtitle of the shortcut.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: The title of the shortcut.

name: AdwShortcutsSection
doc: An object representing a section in [class@ShortcutsDialog].

properties.name: title
properties.doc: The title of the section, can be `NULL`.

doc: A paintable showing a loading spinner. `AdwSpinnerPaintable` size varies depending on the available space, but is capped at 64×64 pixels. To be able to animate, `AdwSpinnerPaintable` needs a widget.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The widget the spinner uses for frame clock.

name: AdwSplitButton
doc: A combined button and dropdown widget. `AdwSplitButton` is typically used to present a set of actions in a menu, but allow access to one of them with a single click. The API is very similar to [class@Gtk.Button] and [class@Gtk.MenuButton], see their documentation for details. ## CSS nodes ``` splitbutton[.image-button][.text-button] ├── button │ ╰── ├── separator ╰── menubutton ╰── button.toggle ╰── arrow ``` `AdwSplitButton`'s CSS node is called `splitbutton`.

name: AdwSpringAnimation
doc:
A spring-based [class@Animation].

`AdwSpringAnimation` implements an animation driven by a physical model of a
spring described by [struct@SpringParams], with a resting position in
[property@SpringAnimation:value-to], stretched to
[property@SpringAnimation:value-from].

Since the animation is physically simulated, spring animations don't have a
fixed duration.

properties.name: clamp
properties.doc: Whether the animation should be clamped.

properties.name: epsilon
properties.doc: Precision of the spring.

properties.name: estimated-duration
properties.doc: Estimated duration of the animation, in milliseconds.

properties.name: initial-velocity
properties.doc: The initial velocity to start the animation with.

properties.name: spring-params
properties.doc: Physical parameters describing the spring.

properties.name: value-from
properties.doc: The value to animate from.

properties.name: value-to
properties.doc: The value to animate to.

properties.name: velocity
properties.doc: Current velocity of the animation.

properties.name: follow-enable-animations-setting
properties.doc:
Whether to skip the animation when animations are globally disabled.

The default behavior is to skip the animation.

properties.name: state
properties.doc: The animation state.

properties.name: target
properties.doc: The target to animate.

properties.name: value
properties.doc: The current value of the animation.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc:
The animation widget.

It provides the frame clock for the animation.

signals.name: done
signals.doc:
This signal is emitted when the animation has been completed, either on its
own or via calling [method@Animation.skip].

name: AdwSqueezer
doc: A best fit container. The `AdwSqueezer` widget is a container which only shows the first of its children that fits in the available size.

properties.name: allow-none
properties.doc:
Whether to allow squeezing beyond the last child's minimum size.

If set to `TRUE`, the squeezer can shrink to the point where no child can
be shown.

properties.name: homogeneous
properties.doc:
Whether all children have the same size for the opposite orientation.

For example, if a squeezer is horizontal and is homogeneous, it will
request the same height for all its children.

properties.name: interpolate-size
properties.doc:
Whether the squeezer interpolates its size when changing the visible child.

If `TRUE`, the squeezer will interpolate its size between the one of the
previous visible child and the one of the new visible child, according to
the set transition duration and the orientation, e.g.

properties.name: pages
properties.doc:
A selection model with the squeezer's pages.

This can be used to keep an up-to-date view.

properties.name: switch-threshold-policy
properties.doc:
The switch threshold policy.

Determines when the squeezer will switch children.

If set to `ADW_FOLD_THRESHOLD_POLICY_MINIMUM`, it will only switch when the
visible child cannot fit anymore.

properties.name: transition-duration
properties.doc: The transition animation duration, in milliseconds.

properties.name: transition-running
properties.doc: Whether a transition is currently running.

properties.name: transition-type
properties.doc: The type of animation used for transitions between children.

properties.name: visible-child
properties.doc: The currently visible child.

properties.doc: The horizontal alignment, from 0 (start) to 1 (end).

name: AdwStyleManager
doc: A class for managing application-wide styling.

properties.name: accent-color
properties.doc: The current system accent color.

properties.name: accent-color-rgba
properties.doc:
The current system accent color as a `GdkRGBA`.

Equivalent to calling [func@AccentColor.to_rgba] on the value of
[property@StyleManager:accent-color].

This is a background color.

properties.name: color-scheme
properties.doc:
The requested application color scheme.

The effective appearance will be decided based on the application color
scheme and the system preferred color scheme.

properties.name: dark
properties.doc: Whether the application is using dark appearance.

properties.name: display
properties.doc: The display the style manager is associated with.

properties.name: document-font-name
properties.doc:
The system document font.

The font is in the same format as [property@Gtk.Settings:gtk-font-name],
e.g.

properties.name: high-contrast
properties.doc: Whether the application is using high contrast appearance.

properties.name: monospace-font-name
properties.doc:
The system monospace font.

The font is in the same format as [property@Gtk.Settings:gtk-font-name],
e.g.

properties.name: system-supports-accent-colors
properties.doc:
Whether the system supports accent colors.

This property can be used to check if the current environment provides an
accent color preference.

properties.name: system-supports-color-schemes
properties.doc:
Whether the system supports color schemes.

This property can be used to check if the current environment provides a
color scheme preference.

properties.name: yaru-accent
properties.doc: The Yaru accent color used by the application, if any.

name: AdwSwipeTracker
doc:
A swipe tracker used in [class@Carousel], [class@NavigationView] and
[class@OverlaySplitView].

The `AdwSwipeTracker` object can be used for implementing widgets with swipe
gestures.

properties.name: allow-long-swipes
properties.doc: Whether to allow swiping for more than one snap point at a time.

properties.name: allow-mouse-drag
properties.doc: Whether to allow dragging with mouse pointer.

properties.name: allow-window-handle
properties.doc: Whether to allow touchscreen swiping from `GtkWindowHandle`.

properties.name: enabled
properties.doc:
Whether the swipe tracker is enabled.

When it's not enabled, no events will be processed.

properties.name: lower-overshoot
properties.doc: Whether to allow swiping past the first available snap point.

properties.name: reversed
properties.doc: Whether to reverse the swipe direction.

properties.name: swipeable
properties.doc: The widget the swipe tracker is attached to.

properties.name: upper-overshoot
properties.doc: Whether to allow swiping past the last available snap point.

signals.name: begin-swipe
signals.doc:
This signal is emitted right before a swipe will be started, after the
drag threshold has been passed.

signals.name: end-swipe
signals.doc:
This signal is emitted as soon as the gesture has stopped.

The user is expected to animate the deceleration from the current progress
value to @to with an animation using @velocity as the initial velocity,
provided in pixels per second.

signals.name: prepare
signals.doc: This signal is emitted when a possible swipe is detected.

signals.name: update-swipe
signals.doc: This signal is emitted every time the progress value changes.

is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

`AdwTabView` is a container which shows one child at a time.

not loading, doesn't have an icon and an indicator.

name: AdwTimedAnimation
doc:
A time-based [class@Animation].

`AdwTimedAnimation` implements a simple animation interpolating the given
value from [property@TimedAnimation:value-from] to
[property@TimedAnimation:value-to] over
[property@TimedAnimation:duration] milliseconds using the curve described by
[property@TimedAnimation:easing].

If [property@TimedAnimation:reverse] is set to `TRUE`, `AdwTimedAnimation`
will instead animate from [property@TimedAnimation:value-to] to
[property@TimedAnimation:value-from], and the easing curve will be inverted.

The animation can repeat a certain amount of times, or endlessly, depending
on the [property@TimedAnimation:repeat-count] value.

properties.name: alternate
properties.doc: Whether the animation changes direction on every iteration.

properties.name: duration
properties.doc: Duration of the animation, in milliseconds.

properties.name: easing
properties.doc: Easing function used in the animation.

properties.name: repeat-count
properties.doc: Number of times the animation will play.

properties.name: reverse
properties.doc: Whether the animation plays backwards.

properties.name: value-from
properties.doc: The value to animate from.

properties.name: value-to
properties.doc: The value to animate to.

properties.name: follow-enable-animations-setting
properties.doc:
Whether to skip the animation when animations are globally disabled.

The default behavior is to skip the animation.

properties.name: state
properties.doc: The animation state.

properties.name: target
properties.doc: The target to animate.

properties.name: value
properties.doc: The current value of the animation.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc:
The animation widget.

It provides the frame clock for the animation.

signals.name: done
signals.doc:
This signal is emitted when the animation has been completed, either on its
own or via calling [method@Animation.skip].

name: AdwToast
doc: A helper object for [class@ToastOverlay]. Toasts are meant to be passed into [method@ToastOverlay.add_toast] as follows: ```c adw_toast_overlay_add_toast (overlay, adw_toast_new (_("Simple Toast"))); ``` Toasts always have a close button.

properties.name: action-name
properties.doc: The name of the associated action.

properties.name: action-target
properties.doc: The parameter for action invocations.

properties.name: button-label
properties.doc: The label to show on the button.

properties.name: custom-title
properties.doc:
The custom title widget.

It will be displayed instead of the title if set.

properties.name: priority
properties.doc: The priority of the toast.

properties.name: timeout
properties.doc: The timeout of the toast, in seconds.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: The title of the toast.

properties.doc: Whether to use Pango markup for the toast title.

signals.name: button-clicked
signals.doc: Emitted after the button has been clicked.

signals.name: dismissed
signals.doc: Emitted when the toast has been dismissed.

name: AdwToastOverlay
doc: A widget showing toasts above its content. Much like [class@Gtk.Overlay], `AdwToastOverlay` is a container with a single main child, on top of which it can display a [class@Toast], overlaid. Toasts can be shown with [method@ToastOverlay.add_toast]. Use [method@ToastOverlay.dismiss_all] to dismiss all toasts at once, or [method@Toast.dismiss] to dismiss a single toast. See [class@Toast] for details. ## CSS nodes ``` toastoverlay ├── [child] ├── toast ┊ ├── widget ┊ │ ├── [label.heading] │ ╰── [custom title] ├── [button] ╰── button.circular.flat ``` `AdwToastOverlay`'s CSS node is called `toastoverlay`.

properties.name: bottom-bar-height
properties.doc: The current bottom bar height.

properties.name: bottom-bar-style
properties.doc:
Appearance of the bottom bars.

If set to `ADW_TOOLBAR_FLAT`, bottom bars are flat and scrolling content

name: AdwViewStack
doc:
A view container for [class@ViewSwitcher].

`AdwViewStack` is a container which only shows one page at a time.
It is typically used to hold an application's main views.

It doesn't provide a way to transition between pages.
Instead, a separate widget such as [class@ViewSwitcher] can be used with
`AdwViewStack` to provide this functionality.

`AdwViewStack` pages can have a title, an icon, an attention request, and a
numbered badge that [class@ViewSwitcher] will use to let users identify which
page is which.

properties.name: enable-transitions
properties.doc: Whether the stack uses a crossfade transition between pages.

properties.name: hhomogeneous
properties.doc: Whether the stack is horizontally homogeneous.

properties.doc:
A selection model with the stack's pages.

This can be used to keep an up-to-date view.

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

name: AdwWindow
doc: A freeform window. The `AdwWindow` widget is a subclass of [class@Gtk.Window] which has no titlebar area.

properties.name: adaptive-preview
properties.doc:
Whether adaptive preview is currently open.

Adaptive preview is a debugging tool used for testing the window
contents at specific screen sizes, simulating mobile environment.

Adaptive preview can always be accessed from inspector.

properties.name: content
properties.doc: The content widget.

properties.name: current-breakpoint
properties.doc: The current breakpoint.

properties.name: dialogs
properties.doc: The open dialogs.

properties.name: visible-dialog
properties.doc: The currently visible dialog

properties.name: application

properties.name: default-width

properties.name: deletable
properties.doc: Whether the window frame should have a close button.

properties.name: destroy-with-parent
properties.doc: If this window should be destroyed when the parent is destroyed.

properties.name: display
properties.doc: The display that will display this window.

properties.name: focus-visible
properties.doc: Whether 'focus rectangles' are currently visible in this window.

properties.name: fullscreened
properties.doc: Whether the window is fullscreen.

properties.name: gravity
properties.doc: The gravity to use when resizing the window programmatically.

properties.name: handle-menubar-accel
properties.doc:
Whether the window frame should handle <kbd>F10</kbd> for activating
menubars.

properties.name: hide-on-close
properties.doc:
If this window should be hidden instead of destroyed when the user clicks
the close button.

name: AdwWrapBox
doc: A box-like widget that can wrap into multiple lines. `AdwWrapBox` is similar to [class@Gtk.Box], but can wrap lines when the widgets cannot fit otherwise.

properties.name: align
properties.doc:
The alignment of the children within each line.

0 means the children are placed at the start of the line, 1 means they are
placed at the end of the line.

properties.name: child-spacing
properties.doc: The spacing between widgets on the same line.

properties.name: child-spacing-unit
properties.doc: The length unit for child spacing.

properties.name: justify
properties.doc:
Determines whether and how each complete line should be stretched to fill
the entire widget.

If set to `ADW_JUSTIFY_FILL`, each widget in the line will be stretched,
keeping consistent spacing, so that the line fills the entire widget.

If set to `ADW_JUSTIFY_SPREAD`, the spacing between widgets will be
increased, keeping widget sizes intact.

properties.name: justify-last-line
properties.doc: Whether the last line should be stretched to fill the entire widget.

properties.name: line-homogeneous
properties.doc: Whether all lines should take the same amount of space.

properties.name: line-spacing
properties.doc: The spacing between lines.

properties.name: line-spacing-unit
properties.doc: The length unit for line spacing.

properties.name: natural-line-length
properties.doc: Determines the natural size for each line.

properties.name: natural-line-length-unit
properties.doc: The length unit for natural line length.

properties.name: pack-direction
properties.doc: The direction children are packed in each line.

properties.name: wrap-policy
properties.doc: The policy for line wrapping.

properties.name: wrap-reverse
properties.doc:
Whether wrap direction should be reversed.

By default, lines wrap downwards in a horizontal box, and towards the end
in a vertical box.

properties.doc:

name: GtkAboutDialog
doc:
Displays information about a program.

The shown information includes the programs' logo, name, copyright,
website and license.

properties.name: artists
properties.doc: The people who contributed artwork to the program.

properties.name: authors
properties.doc: The authors of the program.

properties.name: comments
properties.doc: Comments about the program.

properties.name: copyright
properties.doc: Copyright information for the program.

properties.name: documenters
properties.doc: The people documenting the program.

properties.name: license
properties.doc:
The license of the program, as free-form text.

This string is displayed in a text view in a secondary dialog, therefore
it is fine to use a long multi-paragraph text.

properties.name: license-type
properties.doc: The license of the program.

properties.name: logo
properties.doc: A logo for the about box.

properties.name: logo-icon-name
properties.doc: A named icon to use as the logo for the about box.

properties.name: program-name
properties.doc: The name of the program.

properties.name: system-information
properties.doc:
Information about the system on which the program is running.

This information is displayed in a separate page, therefore it is fine
to use a long multi-paragraph text.

properties.name: translator-credits
properties.doc: Credits to the translators.

properties.name: version
properties.doc: The version of the program.

properties.name: website
properties.doc: The URL for the link to the website of the program.

properties.name: website-label
properties.doc: The label for the link to the website of the program.

properties.name: wrap-license
properties.doc: Whether to wrap the text in the license dialog.

properties.name: application
properties.doc: The `GtkApplication` associated with the window.

properties.name: child
properties.doc: The child widget.

properties.name: decorated
properties.doc: Whether the window should have a frame (also known as *decorations*).

properties.name: default-height
properties.doc: The default height of the window.

properties.name: default-widget
properties.doc: The default widget.

properties.name: default-width
properties.doc: The default width of the window.

properties.name: deletable
properties.doc: Whether the window frame should have a close button.

name: GtkActivateAction
doc: Activates a widget.

name: GtkAdjustment
doc:
A model for a numeric value.

The `GtkAdjustment` has an associated lower and upper bound.
It also contains step and page increments, and a page size.

Adjustments are used within several GTK widgets, including
[class@Gtk.SpinButton], [class@Gtk.Viewport], [class@Gtk.Scrollbar]
and [class@Gtk.Scale].

The `GtkAdjustment` object does not update the value itself.

properties.name: lower
properties.doc: The minimum value of the adjustment.

properties.name: page-increment
properties.doc: The page increment of the adjustment.

properties.name: page-size
properties.doc:
The page size of the adjustment.

Note that the page-size is irrelevant and should be set to zero
if the adjustment is used for a simple scalar value, e.g.

properties.name: step-increment
properties.doc: The step increment of the adjustment.

properties.name: upper
properties.doc: The maximum value of the adjustment.

properties.name: value
properties.doc: The value of the adjustment.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted when one or more of the `GtkAdjustment` properties have been
changed.

signals.name: value-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the value has been changed.

name: GtkAlertDialog
doc: Collects the arguments that are needed to present a message to the user.

properties.name: buttons
properties.doc: Labels for buttons to show in the alert.

properties.name: cancel-button
properties.doc:
Determines what happens when the <kbd>Escape</kbd> key is pressed
while the alert is shown.

If this property holds the index of a button in [property@Gtk.AlertDialog:buttons],
then pressing Escape is treated as if that button was pressed.

properties.name: default-button
properties.doc:
Determines what happens when the <kbd>Return</kbd> key is pressed
while the alert is shown.

If this property holds the index of a button in [property@Gtk.AlertDialog:buttons],
then pressing Return is treated as if that button was pressed.

properties.name: detail
properties.doc: The detail text for the alert.

properties.name: message
properties.doc: The message for the alert.

properties.name: modal
properties.doc: Whether the alert is modal.

name: GtkAlternativeTrigger
doc: Combines two shortcut triggers.

properties.name: first
properties.doc: The first `GtkShortcutTrigger` to check.

properties.name: second
properties.doc: The second `GtkShortcutTrigger` to check.

name: GtkAnyFilter
doc: Matches an item when at least one of its filters matches.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the filter changed.

Users of the filter should then check items again via
[method@Gtk.Filter.match].

`GtkFilterListModel` handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, not all items need
to be checked, but only some.

name: GtkAppChooserButton
doc: The `GtkAppChooserButton` lets the user select an application. Initially, a `GtkAppChooserButton` selects the first application in its list, which will either be the most-recently used application or, if [property@Gtk.AppChooserButton:show-default-item] is %TRUE, the default application. The list of applications shown in a `GtkAppChooserButton` includes the recommended applications for the given content type.

properties.name: heading
properties.doc:
The text to show at the top of the dialog that can be
opened from the button.

properties.name: modal
properties.doc: Whether the app chooser dialog should be modal.

properties.doc:

name: GtkAppChooserWidget
doc:
`GtkAppChooserWidget` is a widget for selecting applications.

It is the main building block for [class@Gtk.AppChooserDialog].
Most applications only need to use the latter; but you can use
this widget as part of a larger widget if you have special needs.

`GtkAppChooserWidget` offers detailed control over what applications
are shown, using the
[property@Gtk.AppChooserWidget:show-default],
[property@Gtk.AppChooserWidget:show-recommended],
[property@Gtk.AppChooserWidget:show-fallback],
[property@Gtk.AppChooserWidget:show-other] and
[property@Gtk.AppChooserWidget:show-all] properties.

properties.name: default-text
properties.doc:
The text that appears in the widget when there are no applications
for the given content type.

properties.name: show-all
properties.doc:
If %TRUE, the app chooser presents all applications
in a single list, without subsections for default,
recommended or related applications.

properties.name: show-default
properties.doc:
Determines whether the app chooser should show the default
handler for the content type in a separate section.

properties.name: show-fallback
properties.doc:
Determines whether the app chooser should show a section
for fallback applications.

properties.name: show-other
properties.doc:
Determines whether the app chooser should show a section
for other applications.

properties.name: show-recommended

Determines whether the app chooser should show a section

name: GtkApplication
doc:
A high-level API for writing applications.

`GtkApplication` supports many aspects of writing a GTK application
in a convenient fashion, without enforcing a one-size-fits-all model.

Currently, it handles GTK initialization, application uniqueness, session
management, provides some basic scriptability and desktop shell integration
by exporting actions and menus and manages a list of toplevel windows whose
life-cycle is automatically tied to the life-cycle of your application.

While `GtkApplication` works fine with plain [class@Gtk.Window]s,
it is recommended to use it together with [class@Gtk.ApplicationWindow].

## Automatic resources

`GtkApplication` will automatically load menus from the `GtkBuilder`
resource located at "gtk/menus.ui", relative to the application's
resource base path (see [method@Gio.Application.set_resource_base_path]).
The menu with the ID "menubar" is taken as the application's
menubar.

properties.name: active-window
properties.doc: The currently focused window of the application.

properties.name: menubar
properties.doc: The menu model to be used for the application's menu bar.

properties.name: register-session
properties.doc: Set this property to true to register with the session manager.

properties.name: screensaver-active
properties.doc:
This property is true if GTK believes that the screensaver
is currently active.

signals.name: query-end
signals.doc: Emitted when the session manager is about to end the session.

signals.name: window-added
signals.doc: Emitted when a window is added to an application.

signals.name: window-removed
signals.doc: Emitted when a window is removed from an application.

name: GtkApplicationWindow
doc:
A `GtkWindow` subclass that integrates with `GtkApplication`.

Notably, `GtkApplicationWindow` can handle an application menubar.

This class implements the [iface@Gio.ActionGroup] and [iface@Gio.ActionMap]
interfaces, to let you add window-specific actions that will be exported
by the associated [class@Gtk.Application], together with its application-wide
actions.

signals.name: apply
signals.doc:
Emitted when the apply button is clicked.

The default behavior of the `GtkAssistant` is to switch to the page
after the current page, unless the current page is the last one.

A handler for the ::apply signal should carry out the actions for
which the wizard has collected data.

signals.name: close
signals.doc:
Emitted either when the close button of a summary page is clicked,

Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

name: GtkBoxLayout
doc:
Arranges children in a single row or column.

Whether it is a row or column depends on the value of its
[property@Gtk.Orientable:orientation] property.

properties.name: baseline-child
properties.doc:
The child that determines the baseline of the box
in vertical layout.

If the child does baseline positioning, then its baseline
is lined up with the baseline of the box.

properties.name: baseline-position
properties.doc:
The position of the allocated baseline within the extra space
allocated to each child.

properties.name: homogeneous
properties.doc:
Whether the box layout should distribute the available space
equally among the children.

properties.name: spacing
properties.doc: The space to put between the children.

name: GtkBuilder
doc:
Reads XML descriptions of a user interface and instantiates the described objects.

To create a `GtkBuilder` from a user interface description, call
[ctor@Gtk.Builder.new_from_file], [ctor@Gtk.Builder.new_from_resource]
or [ctor@Gtk.Builder.new_from_string].

In the (unusual) case that you want to add user interface
descriptions from multiple sources to the same `GtkBuilder` you can
call [ctor@Gtk.Builder.new] to get an empty builder and populate it by
(multiple) calls to [method@Gtk.Builder.add_from_file],
[method@Gtk.Builder.add_from_resource] or
[method@Gtk.Builder.add_from_string].

A `GtkBuilder` holds a reference to all objects that it has constructed
and drops these references when it is finalized.

properties.name: current-object
properties.doc: The object the builder is evaluating for.

properties.name: scope
properties.doc: The scope the builder is operating in

properties.name: translation-domain
properties.doc:
The translation domain used when translating property values that
have been marked as translatable.

name: GtkBuilderCScope
doc:
A `GtkBuilderScope` implementation for the C language.

`GtkBuilderCScope` instances use symbols explicitly added to @builder
with prior calls to [method@Gtk.BuilderCScope.add_callback_symbol].
If developers want to do that, they are encouraged to create their
own scopes for that purpose.

In the case that symbols are not explicitly added; GTK will uses
`GModule`’s introspective features (by opening the module %NULL) to
look at the application’s symbol table.

name: GtkBuilderListItemFactory
doc:
Creates widgets by instantiating `GtkBuilder` UI templates.

The templates must extend the class that the parent widget expects.
For example, a factory provided to [property@Gtk.ListView:factory] must have
a template that extends [class@Gtk.ListItem].

Templates typically use [class@Gtk.Expression] to obtain data from the items
in the model.

Example:
```xml
  <interface>
    <template class="GtkListItem">
      <property name="child">
        <object class="GtkLabel">
          <property name="xalign">0</property>
          <binding name="label">
            <lookup name="name" type="SettingsKey">
              <lookup name="item">GtkListItem</lookup>
            </lookup>
          </binding>
        </object>
      </property>
    </template>
  </interface>
```

A common approach is to embed such templates as CDATA marked sections into
a surrounding UI file.

name: GtkCellArea
doc:
An abstract class for laying out `GtkCellRenderer`s

The `GtkCellArea` is an abstract class for [iface@Gtk.CellLayout]
widgets (also referred to as "layouting widgets") to interface with
an arbitrary number of [class@Gtk.CellRenderer]s and interact with the user
for a given [iface@Gtk.TreeModel] row.

The cell area handles events, focus navigation, drawing and
size requests and allocations for a given row of data.

Usually users dont have to interact with the `GtkCellArea` directly
unless they are implementing a cell-layouting widget themselves.

## Requesting area sizes

As outlined in
[GtkWidget’s geometry management section](class.Widget.html#height-for-width-geometry-management),
GTK uses a height-for-width
geometry management system to compute the sizes of widgets and user
interfaces.

properties.name: edit-widget
properties.doc:
The widget currently editing the edited cell

This property is read-only and only changes as
a result of a call gtk_cell_area_activate_cell().

properties.name: edited-cell
properties.doc:
The cell in the area that is currently edited

This property is read-only and only changes as
a result of a call gtk_cell_area_activate_cell().

properties.name: focus-cell
properties.doc: The cell in the area that currently has focus

signals.name: add-editable
signals.doc:
Indicates that editing has started on @renderer and that @editable
should be added to the owning cell-layouting widget at @cell_area.

signals.name: apply-attributes
signals.doc: This signal is emitted whenever applying attributes to @area from @model

signals.name: focus-changed
signals.doc: Indicates that focus changed on this @area.

signals.name: remove-editable
signals.doc:
Indicates that editing finished on @renderer and that @editable
should be removed from the owning cell-layouting widget.

name: GtkCellAreaBox
doc:
A cell area that renders GtkCellRenderers into a row or a column

The `GtkCellAreaBox` renders cell renderers into a row or a column
depending on its `GtkOrientation`.

GtkCellAreaBox uses a notion of packing.

properties.doc: The amount of space to reserve between cells.

This property is read-only and only changes as
a result of a call gtk_cell_area_activate_cell().

This property is read-only and only changes as

name: GtkCellAreaContext
doc:
Stores geometrical information for a series of rows in a GtkCellArea

The `GtkCellAreaContext` object is created by a given `GtkCellArea`
implementation via its `GtkCellAreaClass.create_context()` virtual
method and is used to store cell sizes and alignments for a series of
`GtkTreeModel` rows that are requested and rendered in the same context.

`GtkCellLayout` widgets can create any number of contexts in which to
request and render groups of data rows.

properties.name: area
properties.doc: The `GtkCellArea` this context was created by

properties.name: minimum-height
properties.doc:
The minimum height for the `GtkCellArea` in this context
for all `GtkTreeModel` rows that this context was requested
for using gtk_cell_area_get_preferred_height().

properties.name: minimum-width
properties.doc:
The minimum width for the `GtkCellArea` in this context
for all `GtkTreeModel` rows that this context was requested
for using gtk_cell_area_get_preferred_width().

properties.name: natural-height
properties.doc:
The natural height for the `GtkCellArea` in this context
for all `GtkTreeModel` rows that this context was requested
for using gtk_cell_area_get_preferred_height().

properties.name: natural-width
properties.doc:
The natural width for the `GtkCellArea` in this context
for all `GtkTreeModel` rows that this context was requested
for using gtk_cell_area_get_preferred_width().

name: GtkCellRendererProgress
doc:
Renders numbers as progress bars

`GtkCellRendererProgress` renders a numeric value as a progress par in a cell.

properties.name: inverted
properties.doc: Whether progess is inverted.

properties.name: pulse
properties.doc:
Setting this to a non-negative value causes the cell renderer to
enter "activity mode", where a block bounces back and forth to
indicate that some progress is made, without specifying exactly how
much.

Each increment of the property causes the block to move by a little
bit.

To indicate that the activity has not started yet, set the property
to zero.

properties.name: text
properties.doc:
The "text" property determines the label which will be drawn
over the progress bar.

properties.name: text-xalign
properties.doc:
The "text-xalign" property controls the horizontal alignment of the
text in the progress bar.

properties.name: text-yalign
properties.doc:
The "text-yalign" property controls the vertical alignment of the
text in the progress bar.

properties.name: value
properties.doc:
The "value" property determines the percentage to which the
progress bar will be "filled in".

name: GtkCellView
doc:
A widget displaying a single row of a GtkTreeModel

A `GtkCellView` displays a single row of a `GtkTreeModel` using a `GtkCellArea`
and `GtkCellAreaContext`.

properties.name: cell-area
properties.doc:
The `GtkCellArea` rendering cells

If no area is specified when creating the cell view with gtk_cell_view_new_with_context()
a horizontally oriented `GtkCellArea`Box will be used.

properties.name: cell-area-context
properties.doc: The `GtkCellAreaContext` used to compute the geometry of the cell view.

properties.name: draw-sensitive
properties.doc:
Whether all cells should be draw as sensitive for this view regardless
of the actual cell properties (used to make menus with submenus appear
sensitive when the items in submenus might be insensitive).

properties.name: fit-model
properties.doc:
Whether the view should request enough space to always fit
the size of every row in the model (used by the combo box to
ensure the combo box size doesn't change when different items
are selected).

properties.name: model
properties.doc:
The model for cell view

since 2.10

properties.name: can-focus
properties.doc:
Whether the widget or any of its descendents can accept
the input focus.

properties.name: can-target
properties.doc: Whether the widget can receive pointer events.

properties.name: css-classes
properties.doc: A list of css classes applied to this widget.

properties.name: css-name
properties.doc: The name of this widget in the CSS tree.

properties.name: cursor
properties.doc: The cursor used by @widget.

properties.name: focus-on-click
properties.doc: Whether the widget should grab focus when it is clicked with the mouse.

properties.name: focusable
properties.doc: Whether this widget itself will accept the input focus.

properties.name: halign
properties.doc: How to distribute horizontal space if widget gets extra space.

properties.name: has-default
properties.doc: Whether the widget is the default widget.

properties.name: has-focus
properties.doc: Whether the widget has the input focus.

properties.name: has-tooltip
properties.doc:
Enables or disables the emission of the [signal@Gtk.Widget::query-tooltip]
signal on @widget.

properties.name: height-request
properties.doc: Overrides for height request of the widget.

properties.name: hexpand
properties.doc: Whether to expand horizontally.

properties.name: hexpand-set
properties.doc: Whether to use the `hexpand` property.

properties.name: layout-manager
properties.doc:
The [class@Gtk.LayoutManager] instance to use to compute
the preferred size of the widget, and allocate its children.

properties.name: limit-events
properties.doc:
Makes this widget act like a modal dialog, with respect to
event delivery.

Global event controllers will not handle events with targets
inside the widget, unless they are set up to ignore propagation
limits.

properties.name: margin-bottom
properties.doc: Margin on bottom side of widget.

properties.name: margin-end
properties.doc: Margin on end of widget, horizontally.

properties.name: margin-start
properties.doc: Margin on start of widget, horizontally.

properties.name: margin-top
properties.doc: Margin on top side of widget.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the widget.

properties.name: opacity
properties.doc: The requested opacity of the widget.

properties.name: overflow
properties.doc: How content outside the widget's content area is treated.

properties.name: parent
properties.doc: The parent widget of this widget.

properties.name: receives-default
properties.doc: Whether the widget will receive the default action when it is focused.

properties.name: root
properties.doc: The `GtkRoot` widget of the widget tree containing this widget.

properties.name: scale-factor
properties.doc: The scale factor of the widget.

properties.name: sensitive
properties.doc: Whether the widget responds to input.

properties.name: tooltip-markup
properties.doc:
Sets the text of tooltip to be the given string, which is marked up
with Pango markup.

properties.name: tooltip-text
properties.doc: Sets the text of tooltip to be the given string.

properties.name: valign
properties.doc: How to distribute vertical space if widget gets extra space.

properties.name: vexpand
properties.doc: Whether to expand vertically.

properties.name: vexpand-set
properties.doc: Whether to use the `vexpand` property.

properties.name: visible
properties.doc: Whether the widget is visible.

properties.name: width-request
properties.doc: Overrides for width request of the widget.

signals.name: move-focus

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

doc: Arranges three children in a row, keeping the middle child centered as well as possible. To add children to `GtkCenterBox`, use [method@Gtk.CenterBox.set_start_widget], [method@Gtk.CenterBox.set_center_widget] and [method@Gtk.CenterBox.set_end_widget]. name: GtkCheckButton
doc: Places a label next to an indicator. A `GtkCheckButton` is created by calling either [ctor@Gtk.CheckButton.new] or [ctor@Gtk.CheckButton.new_with_label]. The state of a `GtkCheckButton` can be set specifically using [method@Gtk.CheckButton.set_active], and retrieved using [method@Gtk.CheckButton.get_active]. # Inconsistent state In addition to "on" and "off", check buttons can be an "in between" state that is neither on nor off.

properties.name: active
properties.doc: If the check button is active.

properties.name: child
properties.doc: The child widget.

properties.name: group
properties.doc: The check button whose group this widget belongs to.

properties.name: inconsistent
properties.doc: If the check button is in an “in between” state.

properties.name: label
properties.doc: Text of the label inside the check button, if it contains a label widget.

doc: A dialog for choosing a color. `GtkColorChooserDialog` implements the [iface@Gtk.ColorChooser] interface and does not provide much API of its own. To create a `GtkColorChooserDialog`, use [ctor@Gtk.ColorChooserDialog.new]. To change the initially selected color, use [method@Gtk.ColorChooser.set_rgba].

name: GtkColumnView
doc:
Presents a large dynamic list of items using multiple columns with headers.

`GtkColumnView` uses the factories of its columns to generate a cell widget for
each column, for each visible item and displays them together as the row for
this item.

The [property@Gtk.ColumnView:show-row-separators] and
[property@Gtk.ColumnView:show-column-separators] properties offer a simple way
to display separators between the rows or columns.

`GtkColumnView` allows the user to select items according to the selection
characteristics of the model.

doc:
Represents items in a cell in [class@Gtk.ColumnView].

The `GtkColumnViewCell`s are managed by the [class@Gtk.ColumnView]
widget (with its factory) and cannot be created by applications, but
they need to be populated by application code.

name: GtkColumnViewSorter
doc:
Sorts [class@Gtk.ColumnView] columns.

The sorter returned by [method@Gtk.ColumnView.get_sorter] is
a `GtkColumnViewSorter`.

In column views, sorting can be configured by associating
sorters with columns, and users can invert sort order by clicking
on column headers.

properties.name: primary-sort-column
properties.doc: The primary sort column.

properties.name: primary-sort-order
properties.doc: The primary sort order.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the sorter changed.

Users of the sorter should then update the sort order
again via gtk_sorter_compare().

[class@Gtk.SortListModel] handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, it may be possible to update
the sort order without a full resorting.

name: GtkComboBox
doc: A `GtkComboBox` is a widget that allows the user to choose from a list of valid choices. The `GtkComboBox` displays the selected choice; when activated, the `GtkComboBox` displays a popup which allows the user to make a new choice. The `GtkComboBox` uses the model-view pattern; the list of valid choices is specified in the form of a tree model, and the display of the choices can be adapted to the data in the model by using cell renderers, as you would in a tree view.

name: GtkComboBoxText
doc: A `GtkComboBoxText` is a simple variant of `GtkComboBox` for text-only use cases. `GtkComboBoxText` hides the model-view complexity of `GtkComboBox`. To create a `GtkComboBoxText`, use [ctor@Gtk.ComboBoxText.new] or [ctor@Gtk.ComboBoxText.new_with_entry]. You can add items to a `GtkComboBoxText` with [method@Gtk.ComboBoxText.append_text], [method@Gtk.ComboBoxText.insert_text] or [method@Gtk.ComboBoxText.prepend_text] and remove options with [method@Gtk.ComboBoxText.remove]. If the `GtkComboBoxText` contains an entry (via the [property@Gtk.ComboBox:has-entry] property), its contents can be retrieved using [method@Gtk.ComboBoxText.get_active_text]. You should not call [method@Gtk.ComboBox.set_model] or attempt to pack more cells into this combo box via its [iface@Gtk.CellLayout] interface. ## GtkComboBoxText as GtkBuildable The `GtkComboBoxText` implementation of the `GtkBuildable` interface supports adding items directly using the `` element and specifying `` elements for each item.

doc:
Describes a constraint between attributes of two widgets,
 expressed as a linear equation.

the natural size of the guide.

name: GtkConstraintLayout
doc:
Uses constraints to describe relations between widgets.

`GtkConstraintLayout` is a layout manager that uses relations between
widget attributes, expressed via [class@Gtk.Constraint] instances, to
measure and allocate widgets.

### How do constraints work

Constraints are objects defining the relationship between attributes
of a widget; you can read the description of the [class@Gtk.Constraint]
class to have a more in depth definition.

By taking multiple constraints and applying them to the children of
a widget using `GtkConstraintLayout`, it's possible to describe
complex layout policies; each constraint applied to a child or to the parent
widgets contributes to the full description of the layout, in terms of
parameters for resolving the value of each attribute.

It is important to note that a layout is defined by the totality of
constraints; removing a child, or a constraint, from an existing layout
without changing the remaining constraints may result in an unstable
or unsolvable layout.

Constraints have an implicit "reading order"; you should start describing
each edge of each child, as well as their relationship with the parent
container, from the top left (or top right, in RTL languages), horizontally
first, and then vertically.

A constraint-based layout with too few constraints can become "unstable",
that is: have more than one solution.

name: GtkConstraintLayoutChild
doc: `GtkLayoutChild` subclass for children in a `GtkConstraintLayout`.

properties.name: child-widget
properties.doc: The widget that is associated to the `GtkLayoutChild` instance.

properties.name: layout-manager
properties.doc: The layout manager that created the `GtkLayoutChild` instance.

name: GtkCssProvider
doc:
A style provider for CSS.

It is able to parse CSS-like input in order to style widgets.

An application can make GTK parse a specific CSS style sheet by calling
[method@Gtk.CssProvider.load_from_file] or
[method@Gtk.CssProvider.load_from_resource]
and adding the provider with [method@Gtk.StyleContext.add_provider] or
[func@Gtk.StyleContext.add_provider_for_display].

In addition, certain files will be read when GTK is initialized.
First, the file `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/gtk-4.0/gtk.css` is loaded if it
exists.

properties.name: prefers-color-scheme
properties.doc:
Define the color scheme used for rendering the user interface.

The UI can be set to either [enum@Gtk.InterfaceColorScheme.LIGHT],
or [enum@Gtk.InterfaceColorScheme.DARK] mode.

properties.name: prefers-contrast
properties.doc: Define the contrast mode to use for the user interface.

signals.name: parsing-error
signals.doc:
Signals that a parsing error occurred.

The expected error values are in the [error@Gtk.CssParserError]
and [enum@Gtk.CssParserWarning] enumerations.

The @path, @line and @position describe the actual location of
the error as accurately as possible.

Parsing errors are never fatal, so the parsing will resume after
the error.

name: GtkCustomFilter
doc: Determines whether to include items with a callback.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the filter changed.

Users of the filter should then check items again via
[method@Gtk.Filter.match].

`GtkFilterListModel` handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, not all items need
to be checked, but only some.

name: GtkCustomLayout

name: GtkCustomSorter
doc: Sorts items via a callback function.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the sorter changed.

Users of the sorter should then update the sort order
again via gtk_sorter_compare().

[class@Gtk.SortListModel] handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, it may be possible to update
the sort order without a full resorting.

name: GtkDialog
doc: Dialogs are a convenient way to prompt the user for a small amount of input. Typical uses are to display a message, ask a question, or anything else that does not require extensive effort on the user’s part. The main area of a `GtkDialog` is called the "content area", and is yours to populate with widgets such a `GtkLabel` or `GtkEntry`, to present your information, questions, or tasks to the user. In addition, dialogs allow you to add "action widgets".

name: GtkDropTarget

properties.name: actions
properties.doc: The `GdkDragActions` that this drop target supports.

properties.name: current-drop
properties.doc: The `GdkDrop` that is currently being performed.

properties.name: drop
properties.doc: The `GdkDrop` that is currently being performed.

properties.name: formats
properties.doc: The `GdkContentFormats` that determine the supported data formats.

properties.name: preload
properties.doc:
Whether the drop data should be preloaded when the pointer is only
hovering over the widget but has not been released.

properties.name: value
properties.doc:
The value for this drop operation.

This is %NULL if the data has not been loaded yet or no drop
operation is going on.

Data may be available before the [signal@Gtk.DropTarget::drop]
signal gets emitted - for example when the [property@Gtk.DropTarget:preload]
property is set.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name for this controller, typically used for debugging purposes.

properties.name: propagation-limit
properties.doc: The limit for which events this controller will handle.

properties.name: propagation-phase
properties.doc: The propagation phase at which this controller will handle events.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The widget receiving the `GdkEvents` that the controller will handle.

signals.name: accept
signals.doc:
Emitted on the drop site when a drop operation is about to begin.

If the drop is not accepted, %FALSE will be returned and the drop target
will ignore the drop.

signals.name: drop
signals.doc:
Emitted on the drop site when the user drops the data onto the widget.

The signal handler must determine whether the pointer position is in
a drop zone or not.

signals.name: enter
signals.doc: Emitted on the drop site when the pointer enters the widget.

signals.name: leave
signals.doc: Emitted on the drop site when the pointer leaves the widget.

signals.name: motion
signals.doc: Emitted while the pointer is moving over the drop target.

name: GtkDropTargetAsync
doc:
An event controller to receive Drag-and-Drop operations, asynchronously.

It is the more complete but also more complex method of handling drop
operations compared to [class@Gtk.DropTarget], and you should only use
it if `GtkDropTarget` doesn't provide all the features you need.

To use a `GtkDropTargetAsync` to receive drops on a widget, you create
a `GtkDropTargetAsync` object, configure which data formats and actions
you support, connect to its signals, and then attach it to the widget
with [method@Gtk.Widget.add_controller].

During a drag operation, the first signal that a `GtkDropTargetAsync`
emits is [signal@Gtk.DropTargetAsync::accept], which is meant to determine
whether the target is a possible drop site for the ongoing drop.

properties.name: actions
properties.doc: The `GdkDragActions` that this drop target supports.

properties.name: formats
properties.doc: The `GdkContentFormats` that determines the supported data formats.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name for this controller, typically used for debugging purposes.

properties.name: propagation-limit
properties.doc: The limit for which events this controller will handle.

properties.name: propagation-phase
properties.doc: The propagation phase at which this controller will handle events.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The widget receiving the `GdkEvents` that the controller will handle.

signals.name: accept
signals.doc:
Emitted on the drop site when a drop operation is about to begin.

If the drop is not accepted, %FALSE will be returned and the drop target
will ignore the drop.

signals.name: drag-enter
signals.doc: Emitted on the drop site when the pointer enters the widget.

signals.name: drag-leave
signals.doc: Emitted on the drop site when the pointer leaves the widget.

signals.name: drag-motion
signals.doc: Emitted while the pointer is moving over the drop target.

signals.name: drop
signals.doc:
Emitted on the drop site when the user drops the data onto the widget.

The signal handler must determine whether the pointer position is in a
drop zone or not.
