name: GtkEntry
doc: A single-line text entry widget. A fairly large set of key bindings are supported by default.

properties.name: activates-default
properties.doc: Whether to activate the default widget when Enter is pressed.

properties.name: attributes
properties.doc:
A list of Pango attributes to apply to the text of the entry.

This is mainly useful to change the size or weight of the text.

The `PangoAttribute`'s @start_index and @end_index must refer to the
[class@Gtk.EntryBuffer] text, i.e.

properties.name: buffer
properties.doc: The buffer object which actually stores the text.

properties.name: completion
properties.doc: The auxiliary completion object to use with the entry.

properties.name: enable-emoji-completion
properties.doc:
Whether to suggest Emoji replacements for :-delimited names
like `:heart:`.

properties.name: extra-menu
properties.doc: A menu model whose contents will be appended to the context menu.

properties.name: has-frame
properties.doc: Whether the entry should draw a frame.

properties.name: im-module
properties.doc:
Which IM (input method) module should be used for this entry.

See [class@Gtk.IMContext].

Setting this to a non-%NULL value overrides the system-wide IM
module setting.

properties.name: input-hints
properties.doc: Additional hints that allow input methods to fine-tune their behavior.

properties.name: input-purpose
properties.doc: The purpose of this text field.

properties.name: invisible-char
properties.doc: The character to use when masking entry contents (“password mode”).

properties.name: invisible-char-set
properties.doc: Whether the invisible char has been set for the `GtkEntry`.

properties.name: max-length
properties.doc: Maximum number of characters for this entry.

properties.name: menu-entry-icon-primary-text
properties.doc:
Text for an item in the context menu to activate the primary icon action.

When the primary icon is activatable and this property has been set, a new entry
in the context menu of this GtkEntry will appear with this text.

properties.name: menu-entry-icon-secondary-text
properties.doc:
Text for an item in the context menu to activate the secondary icon action.

When the primary icon is activatable and this property has been set, a new entry
in the context menu of this GtkEntry will appear with this text.

properties.name: overwrite-mode
properties.doc: If text is overwritten when typing in the `GtkEntry`.

properties.name: placeholder-text
properties.doc:
The text that will be displayed in the `GtkEntry` when it is empty
and unfocused.

properties.name: primary-icon-activatable
properties.doc: Whether the primary icon is activatable.

properties.name: primary-icon-gicon
properties.doc: The `GIcon` to use for the primary icon for the entry.

properties.name: primary-icon-name
properties.doc: The icon name to use for the primary icon for the entry.

properties.name: primary-icon-paintable
properties.doc: A `GdkPaintable` to use as the primary icon for the entry.

properties.name: primary-icon-sensitive
properties.doc:
Whether the primary icon is sensitive.

An insensitive icon appears grayed out.

properties.name: primary-icon-storage-type
properties.doc: The representation which is used for the primary icon of the entry.

properties.name: primary-icon-tooltip-markup
properties.doc: The contents of the tooltip on the primary icon, with markup.

properties.name: primary-icon-tooltip-text
properties.doc: The contents of the tooltip on the primary icon.

properties.name: progress-fraction
properties.doc: The current fraction of the task that's been completed.

properties.name: progress-pulse-step
properties.doc:
The fraction of total entry width to move the progress
bouncing block for each pulse.

properties.name: scroll-offset
properties.doc: Number of pixels of the entry scrolled off the screen to the left.

properties.name: secondary-icon-activatable
properties.doc: Whether the secondary icon is activatable.

properties.name: secondary-icon-gicon
properties.doc: The `GIcon` to use for the secondary icon for the entry.

properties.name: secondary-icon-name
properties.doc: The icon name to use for the secondary icon for the entry.

properties.name: secondary-icon-paintable
properties.doc: A `GdkPaintable` to use as the secondary icon for the entry.

properties.name: secondary-icon-sensitive
properties.doc:
Whether the secondary icon is sensitive.

An insensitive icon appears grayed out.

properties.name: secondary-icon-storage-type
properties.doc: The representation which is used for the secondary icon of the entry.

properties.name: secondary-icon-tooltip-markup
properties.doc: The contents of the tooltip on the secondary icon, with markup.

properties.name: secondary-icon-tooltip-text
properties.doc: The contents of the tooltip on the secondary icon.

properties.name: show-emoji-icon

over an activatable icon.

signals.doc:
Signals that all holders of a reference to the widget should release
the reference that they hold.

signals.doc: Emitted when the text direction of a widget changes.

signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is hidden.

signals.doc: Emitted when a widget is activated via a mnemonic.

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

name: GtkEntryBuffer
doc:
Holds the text that is displayed in a single-line text entry widget.

A single `GtkEntryBuffer` object can be shared by multiple widgets
which will then share the same text content, but not the cursor
position, visibility attributes, icon etc.

`GtkEntryBuffer` may be derived from.

properties.name: length
properties.doc: The length (in characters) of the text in buffer.

properties.name: max-length
properties.doc: The maximum length (in characters) of the text in the buffer.

properties.name: text
properties.doc: The contents of the buffer.

signals.name: deleted-text
signals.doc: The text is altered in the default handler for this signal.

signals.name: inserted-text
signals.doc: This signal is emitted after text is inserted into the buffer.

name: GtkEntryCompletion
doc:
`GtkEntryCompletion` is an auxiliary object to provide completion functionality
for `GtkEntry`.

It implements the [iface@Gtk.CellLayout] interface, to allow the user
to add extra cells to the `GtkTreeView` with completion matches.

“Completion functionality” means that when the user modifies the text
in the entry, `GtkEntryCompletion` checks which rows in the model match
the current content of the entry, and displays a list of matches.
By default, the matching is done by comparing the entry text
case-insensitively against the text column of the model (see
[method@Gtk.EntryCompletion.set_text_column]), but this can be overridden
with a custom match function (see [method@Gtk.EntryCompletion.set_match_func]).

When the user selects a completion, the content of the entry is
updated.

properties.name: cell-area
properties.doc: The `GtkCellArea` used to layout cell renderers in the treeview column.

properties.name: inline-completion
properties.doc:
Determines whether the common prefix of the possible completions
should be inserted automatically in the entry.

properties.name: inline-selection
properties.doc:
Determines whether the possible completions on the popup
will appear in the entry as you navigate through them.

properties.name: minimum-key-length
properties.doc: The minimum key length as set for completion.

properties.name: model
properties.doc: The model used as data source.

properties.name: popup-completion
properties.doc:
Determines whether the possible completions should be
shown in a popup window.

properties.name: popup-set-width
properties.doc:
Determines whether the completions popup window will be
resized to the width of the entry.

properties.name: popup-single-match
properties.doc:
Determines whether the completions popup window will shown
for a single possible completion.

properties.name: text-column
properties.doc: The column of the model containing the strings.

signals.name: cursor-on-match
signals.doc: Emitted when a match from the cursor is on a match of the list.

signals.name: insert-prefix
signals.doc:
Emitted when the inline autocompletion is triggered.

The default behaviour is to make the entry display the
whole prefix and select the newly inserted part.

Applications may connect to this signal in order to insert only a
smaller part of the @prefix into the entry - e.g.

signals.name: match-selected
signals.doc: Emitted when a match from the list is selected.

signals.name: no-matches
signals.doc:
Emitted when the filter model has zero
number of rows in completion_complete method.

name: GtkEventController
doc:
The base class for event controllers.

These are ancillary objects associated to widgets, which react
to `GdkEvents`, and possibly trigger actions as a consequence.

Event controllers are added to a widget with
[method@Gtk.Widget.add_controller].

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name for this controller, typically used for debugging purposes.

properties.name: propagation-limit
properties.doc: The limit for which events this controller will handle.

properties.name: propagation-phase
properties.doc: The propagation phase at which this controller will handle events.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The widget receiving the `GdkEvents` that the controller will handle.

name: GtkEventControllerFocus
doc: Tracks keyboard focus.

properties.name: contains-focus
properties.doc: %TRUE if focus is contained in the controllers widget.

properties.name: is-focus
properties.doc:
%TRUE if focus is in the controllers widget itself,
as opposed to in a descendent widget.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name for this controller, typically used for debugging purposes.

properties.name: propagation-limit
properties.doc: The limit for which events this controller will handle.

properties.name: propagation-phase
properties.doc: The propagation phase at which this controller will handle events.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The widget receiving the `GdkEvents` that the controller will handle.

signals.name: enter
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the focus enters into the widget or one
of its descendents.

Note that this means you may not get an ::enter signal
even though the widget becomes the focus location, in
certain cases (such as when the focus moves from a descendent
of the widget to the widget itself).

signals.name: leave
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the focus leaves the widget hierarchy
that is rooted at the widget that the controller is attached to.

Note that this means you may not get a ::leave signal
even though the focus moves away from the widget, in
certain cases (such as when the focus moves from the widget
to a descendent).

name: GtkEventControllerKey
doc: Provides access to key events.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name for this controller, typically used for debugging purposes.

properties.name: propagation-limit
properties.doc: The limit for which events this controller will handle.

properties.name: propagation-phase
properties.doc: The propagation phase at which this controller will handle events.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The widget receiving the `GdkEvents` that the controller will handle.

signals.name: key-pressed

signals.name: key-released

signals.name: modifiers
signals.doc: Emitted whenever the state of modifier keys and pointer buttons change.

properties.name: propagation-phase

name: GtkEventControllerScroll
doc:
Handles scroll events.

It is capable of handling both discrete and continuous scroll
events from mice or touchpads, abstracting them both with the
[signal@Gtk.EventControllerScroll::scroll] signal.

properties.name: flags
properties.doc: The flags affecting event controller behavior.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name for this controller, typically used for debugging purposes.

properties.name: propagation-limit
properties.doc: The limit for which events this controller will handle.

properties.name: propagation-phase
properties.doc: The propagation phase at which this controller will handle events.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The widget receiving the `GdkEvents` that the controller will handle.

signals.name: decelerate
signals.doc:
Emitted after scroll is finished if the
%GTK_EVENT_CONTROLLER_SCROLL_KINETIC flag is set.

@vel_x and @vel_y express the initial velocity that was
imprinted by the scroll events.

signals.name: scroll
signals.doc:
Signals that the widget should scroll by the
amount specified by @dx and @dy.

signals.name: scroll-begin
signals.doc: Signals that a new scrolling operation has begun.

signals.name: scroll-end

doc: Matches an item when each of its filters matches.

properties.doc: The type of items.

signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the filter changed.

Users of the filter should then check items again via
[method@Gtk.Filter.match].

`GtkFilterListModel` handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, not all items need
to be checked, but only some.

name: GtkExpander
doc: Allows the user to reveal or conceal a child widget. This is similar to the triangles used in a `GtkTreeView`. Normally you use an expander as you would use a frame; you create the child widget and use [method@Gtk.Expander.set_child] to add it to the expander.

name: GtkExpression
doc:
Provides a way to describe references to values.

An important aspect of expressions is that the value can be obtained
from a source that is several steps away.

signals.name: desktop-folder
signals.doc: Emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: down-folder
signals.doc:
Emitted when the user asks for it.

This is a [keybinding signal](class.SignalAction.html).

This is used to make the file chooser go to a child of the
current folder in the file hierarchy.

signals.name: home-folder
signals.doc: Emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: location-popup
signals.doc:
Emitted when the user asks for it.

This is a [keybinding signal](class.SignalAction.html).

This is used to make the file chooser show a "Location" prompt which
the user can use to manually type the name of the file he wishes to select.

The default bindings for this signal are <kbd>Control</kbd>-<kbd>L</kbd>
with a @path string of "" (the empty string).

signals.name: location-popup-on-paste
signals.doc: Emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: location-toggle-popup
signals.doc: Emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: places-shortcut
signals.doc: Emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: quick-bookmark
signals.doc:
Emitted when the user asks for it.

This is a [keybinding signal](class.SignalAction.html).

This is used to make the file chooser switch to the bookmark
specified in the @bookmark_index parameter.

signals.name: recent-shortcut
signals.doc: Emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: search-shortcut
signals.doc: Emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

name: GtkFileDialog
doc: Asynchronous API to present a file chooser dialog.

properties.name: accept-label
properties.doc: Label for the file chooser's accept button.

properties.name: default-filter
properties.doc:
The default filter.

This filter is initially active in the file chooser dialog.

If the default filter is `NULL`, the first filter of [property@Gtk.FileDialog:filters]
is used as the default filter.

properties.name: filters
properties.doc: The list of filters.

properties.name: initial-file
properties.doc: The initial file.

properties.name: initial-folder
properties.doc: The initial folder.

properties.name: initial-name
properties.doc: The initial name.

properties.name: modal
properties.doc: Whether the file chooser dialog is modal.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: A title that may be shown on the file chooser dialog.

name: GtkFileFilter
doc:
Filters files by name or mime type.

`GtkFileFilter` can be used to restrict the files being shown in a
file chooser.

properties.name: mime-types
properties.doc: The MIME types that this filter matches.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The human-readable name of the filter.

properties.name: patterns
properties.doc: The patterns that this filter matches.

properties.name: suffixes
properties.doc: The suffixes that this filter matches.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the filter changed.

Users of the filter should then check items again via
[method@Gtk.Filter.match].

`GtkFilterListModel` handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, not all items need
to be checked, but only some.

name: GtkFileLauncher
doc: Asynchronous API to open a file with an application.

properties.name: always-ask
properties.doc: Whether to ask the user to choose an app for opening the file.

properties.name: file
properties.doc: The file to launch.

properties.name: writable
properties.doc: Whether to make the file writable for the handler.

name: GtkFilter
doc:
Describes the filtering to be performed by a [class@Gtk.FilterListModel].

The model will use the filter to determine if it should include items
or not by calling [method@Gtk.Filter.match] for each item and only
keeping the ones that the function returns true for.

Filters may change what items they match through their lifetime.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the filter changed.

Users of the filter should then check items again via
[method@Gtk.Filter.match].

`GtkFilterListModel` handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, not all items need
to be checked, but only some.

name: GtkFilterListModel
doc:
A list model that filters the elements of another model.

It hides some elements from the underlying model according to
criteria given by a `GtkFilter`.

The model can be set up to do incremental filtering, so that
filtering long lists doesn't block the UI.

properties.name: filter
properties.doc: The filter for this model.

properties.name: incremental
properties.doc: If the model should filter items incrementally.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: model
properties.doc: The model being filtered.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

properties.name: pending
properties.doc: Number of items not yet filtered.

properties.name: watch-items
properties.doc: Monitor the list items for changes.

name: GtkFixed
doc:
Places its child widgets at fixed positions and with fixed sizes.

`GtkFixed` performs no automatic layout management.

For most applications, you should not use this container! It keeps
you from having to learn about the other GTK containers, but it
results in broken applications.

name: GtkFixedLayout
doc:
Places child widgets at fixed positions.

Most applications should never use this layout manager; fixed positioning
and sizing requires constant recalculations on where children need to be
positioned and sized.

name: GtkFixedLayoutChild
doc: `GtkLayoutChild` subclass for children in a `GtkFixedLayout`.

properties.doc: The transform of the child.

properties.name: layout-manager
properties.doc: The layout manager that created the `GtkLayoutChild` instance.

name: GtkFlattenListModel
doc:
A list model that concatenates other list models.

`GtkFlattenListModel` takes a list model containing list models, and flattens
it into a single model.

properties.name: item-type

properties.name: model

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

name: GtkFlowBox

name: GtkGLArea
doc: Allows drawing with OpenGL. `GtkGLArea` sets up its own [class@Gdk.GLContext], and creates a custom GL framebuffer that the widget will do GL rendering onto.

properties.name: allowed-apis
properties.doc: The allowed APIs.

properties.name: api
properties.doc: The API currently in use.

properties.name: auto-render
properties.doc:
If set to %TRUE the ::render signal will be emitted every time
the widget draws.

properties.name: context
properties.doc:
The `GdkGLContext` used by the `GtkGLArea` widget.

The `GtkGLArea` widget is responsible for creating the `GdkGLContext`
instance.

properties.name: has-depth-buffer
properties.doc:
If set to %TRUE the widget will allocate and enable a depth buffer for the
target framebuffer.

Setting this property will enable GL's depth testing as a side effect.

name: GtkGesture
doc:
The base class for gesture recognition.

Although `GtkGesture` is quite generalized to serve as a base for
multi-touch gestures, it is suitable to implement single-touch and
pointer-based gestures (using the special %NULL `GdkEventSequence`
value for these).

The number of touches that a `GtkGesture` need to be recognized is
controlled by the [property@Gtk.Gesture:n-points] property, if a
gesture is keeping track of less or more than that number of sequences,
it won't check whether the gesture is recognized.

As soon as the gesture has the expected number of touches, it will check
regularly if it is recognized, the criteria to consider a gesture as
"recognized" is left to `GtkGesture` subclasses.

A recognized gesture will then emit the following signals:

- [signal@Gtk.Gesture::begin] when the gesture is recognized.
- [signal@Gtk.Gesture::update], whenever an input event is processed.
- [signal@Gtk.Gesture::end] when the gesture is no longer recognized.

## Event propagation

In order to receive events, a gesture needs to set a propagation phase
through [method@Gtk.EventController.set_propagation_phase].

In the capture phase, events are propagated from the toplevel down
to the target widget, and gestures that are attached to containers
above the widget get a chance to interact with the event before it
reaches the target.

In the bubble phase, events are propagated up from the target widget
to the toplevel, and gestures that are attached to containers above
the widget get a chance to interact with events that have not been
handled yet.

## States of a sequence

Whenever input interaction happens, a single event may trigger a cascade
of `GtkGesture`s, both across the parents of the widget receiving the
event and in parallel within an individual widget.

name: GtkGestureSingle
doc:
A `GtkGesture` subclass optimized for singe-touch and mouse gestures.

Under interaction, these gestures stick to the first interacting sequence,
which is accessible through [method@Gtk.GestureSingle.get_current_sequence]
while the gesture is being interacted with.

By default gestures react to both %GDK_BUTTON_PRIMARY and touch events.
[method@Gtk.GestureSingle.set_touch_only] can be used to change the
touch behavior.

name: GtkIMContext
doc:
The interface for GTK input methods.

`GtkIMContext` is used by GTK text input widgets like `GtkText`
to map from key events to Unicode character strings.

An input method may consume multiple key events in sequence before finally
outputting the composed result.

properties.name: input-hints
properties.doc:
Additional hints that allow input methods to fine-tune
their behaviour.

properties.name: input-purpose
properties.doc: The purpose of the text field that the `GtkIMContext is connected to.

signals.name: commit
signals.doc:
The ::commit signal is emitted when a complete input sequence
has been entered by the user.

signals.name: delete-surrounding
signals.doc:
The ::delete-surrounding signal is emitted when the input method
needs to delete all or part of the context surrounding the cursor.

signals.name: preedit-changed
signals.doc:
The ::preedit-changed signal is emitted whenever the preedit sequence
currently being entered has changed.

signals.name: preedit-end
signals.doc:
The ::preedit-end signal is emitted when a preediting sequence
has been completed or canceled.

signals.name: preedit-start
signals.doc:
The ::preedit-start signal is emitted when a new preediting sequence
starts.

signals.name: retrieve-surrounding
signals.doc:
The ::retrieve-surrounding signal is emitted when the input method
requires the context surrounding the cursor.

name: GtkIMContextSimple
doc:
Supports compose sequences, dead keys and numeric Unicode input.

## Compose sequences

`GtkIMContextSimple` reads compose sequences from the first of the
following files that is found: ~/.config/gtk-4.0/Compose, ~/.XCompose,
/usr/share/X11/locale/$locale/Compose (for locales that have a nontrivial
Compose file).

their behaviour.

name: GtkIconPaintable
doc:
Contains information found when looking up an icon in `GtkIconTheme`
or loading it from a file.

properties.name: file
properties.doc: The file representing the icon, if any.

properties.name: icon-name
properties.doc: The icon name that was chosen during lookup.

properties.name: is-symbolic
properties.doc: Whether the icon is symbolic or not.

name: GtkIconTheme
doc:
Loads themed icons.

The main reason for using a name rather than simply providing a filename
is to allow different icons to be used depending on what “icon theme” is
selected by the user.

properties.name: display
properties.doc: The display that this icon theme object is attached to.

properties.name: icon-names
properties.doc: The icon names that are supported by the icon theme.

properties.name: resource-path
properties.doc:
Resource paths that will be looked at when looking for icons,
similar to search paths.

properties.name: search-path
properties.doc:
The search path for this icon theme.

When looking for icons, GTK will search for a subdirectory of
one or more of the directories in the search path with the same
name as the icon theme containing an index.theme file.

properties.name: theme-name
properties.doc: The name of the icon theme that is being used.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the icon theme changes.

name: GtkIconView
doc: `GtkIconView` is a widget which displays data in a grid of icons. `GtkIconView` provides an alternative view on a `GtkTreeModel`. It displays the model as a grid of icons with labels.

properties.name: activate-on-single-click
properties.doc:
The activate-on-single-click property specifies whether the "item-activated" signal
will be emitted after a single click.

properties.name: cell-area
properties.doc: The `GtkCellArea` used to layout cell renderers for this view.

properties.name: column-spacing
properties.doc:
The column-spacing property specifies the space which is inserted between
the columns of the icon view.

properties.name: columns
properties.doc:
The columns property contains the number of the columns in which the
items should be displayed.

properties.name: item-orientation
properties.doc: The item-orientation property specifies how the cells (i.e.

properties.name: item-padding
properties.doc:
The item-padding property specifies the padding around each
of the icon view's item.

properties.name: item-width
properties.doc: The item-width property specifies the width to use for each item.

properties.name: margin
properties.doc:
The margin property specifies the space which is inserted
at the edges of the icon view.

properties.name: markup-column
properties.doc:
The ::markup-column property contains the number of the model column
containing markup information to be displayed.

properties.name: model
properties.doc: The model of the icon view.

properties.name: pixbuf-column
properties.doc:
The ::pixbuf-column property contains the number of the model column
containing the pixbufs which are displayed.

properties.name: reorderable
properties.doc:
The reorderable property specifies if the items can be reordered
by DND.

properties.name: row-spacing
properties.doc:
The row-spacing property specifies the space which is inserted between
the rows of the icon view.

properties.name: selection-mode

The ::selection-mode property specifies the selection mode of
icon view.

properties.name: spacing
properties.doc:
The spacing property specifies the space which is inserted between

name: GtkImage
doc: Displays an image. Various kinds of object can be displayed as an image; most typically, you would load a `GdkTexture` from a file, using the convenience function [ctor@Gtk.Image.new_from_file], for instance: ```c GtkWidget *image = gtk_image_new_from_file ("myfile.png"); ``` If the file isn’t loaded successfully, the image will contain a “broken image” icon similar to that used in many web browsers. If you want to handle errors in loading the file yourself, for example by displaying an error message, then load the image with an image loading framework such as libglycin, then create the `GtkImage` with [ctor@Gtk.Image.new_from_paintable]. Sometimes an application will want to avoid depending on external data files, such as image files.

properties.name: file
properties.doc: A path to the file to display.

name: GtkInfoBar
doc: `GtkInfoBar` can be used to show messages to the user without a dialog. It is often temporarily shown at the top or bottom of a document. In contrast to [class@Gtk.Dialog], which has an action area at the bottom, `GtkInfoBar` has an action area at the side. The API of `GtkInfoBar` is very similar to `GtkDialog`, allowing you to add buttons to the action area with [method@Gtk.InfoBar.add_button] or [ctor@Gtk.InfoBar.new_with_buttons].

Shows text in a predefined area.

You likely want to use `GtkLabel` instead as this widget is intended only

Utility property that sets both the [property@Gtk.Inscription:text] and

This function uses [func@Pango.parse_markup] to parse the markup into text and
attributes.

The widget might turn out larger as well as smaller.

If this property is set to a value smaller than [property@Gtk.Inscription:min-chars],

The widget might turn out larger as well as smaller.

name: GtkLabel
doc: Displays a small amount of text. Most labels are used to label another widget (such as an [class@Entry]). ## Shortcuts and Gestures `GtkLabel` supports the following keyboard shortcuts, when the cursor is visible: - Shift+F10 or Menu opens the context menu. - Ctrl+A or Ctrl+/ selects all. - Ctrl+Shift+A or Ctrl+\ unselects all. Additionally, the following signals have default keybindings: - [signal@Gtk.Label::activate-current-link] - [signal@Gtk.Label::copy-clipboard] - [signal@Gtk.Label::move-cursor] ## Actions `GtkLabel` defines a set of built-in actions: - `clipboard.copy` copies the text to the clipboard. - `clipboard.cut` doesn't do anything, since text in labels can't be deleted. - `clipboard.paste` doesn't do anything, since text in labels can't be edited. - `link.open` opens the link, when activated on a link inside the label. - `link.copy` copies the link to the clipboard, when activated on a link inside the label. - `menu.popup` opens the context menu. - `selection.delete` doesn't do anything, since text in labels can't be deleted. - `selection.select-all` selects all of the text, if the label allows selection. ## CSS nodes ``` label ├── [selection] ├── [link] ┊ ╰── [link] ``` `GtkLabel` has a single CSS node with the name label.

properties.name: attributes
properties.doc: A list of style attributes to apply to the text of the label.

properties.name: ellipsize
properties.doc:
The preferred place to ellipsize the string, if the label does
not have enough room to display the entire string.

Note that setting this property to a value other than
[enum.Pango.EllipsizeMode.none] has the side-effect that the label requests
only enough space to display the ellipsis "...".

properties.name: extra-menu
properties.doc: A menu model whose contents will be appended to the context menu.

properties.name: justify
properties.doc: The alignment of the lines in the text of the label, relative to each other.

properties.name: label
properties.doc:
The contents of the label.

If the string contains Pango markup (see [func@Pango.parse_markup]),
you will have to set the [property@Gtk.Label:use-markup] property to
true in order for the label to display the markup attributes.

properties.name: lines
properties.doc:
The number of lines to which an ellipsized, wrapping label
should display before it gets ellipsized.

properties.name: max-width-chars
properties.doc: The desired maximum width of the label, in characters.

properties.name: mnemonic-keyval
properties.doc: The mnemonic accelerator key for the label.

properties.name: mnemonic-widget
properties.doc: The widget to be activated when the labels mnemonic key is pressed.

properties.name: natural-wrap-mode
properties.doc:
Select the line wrapping for the natural size request.

This only affects the natural size requested.

properties.name: selectable
properties.doc: Whether the label text can be selected with the mouse.

properties.name: single-line-mode
properties.doc:
Whether the label is in single line mode.

In single line mode, the height of the label does not depend on the
actual text, it is always set to ascent + descent of the font.

properties.name: tabs
properties.doc: Custom tabs for this label.

properties.name: use-markup
properties.doc: True if the text of the label includes Pango markup.

properties.name: use-underline
properties.doc:
True if the text of the label indicates a mnemonic with an `_`
before the mnemonic character.

properties.name: width-chars
properties.doc: The desired width of the label, in characters.

properties.name: wrap

signals.name: move-cursor
signals.doc:
Gets emitted when the user initiates a cursor movement.

The `::move-cursor` signal is a [keybinding signal](class.SignalAction.html).
If the cursor is not visible in @entry, this signal causes the viewport to
be moved instead.

Applications should not connect to it, but may emit it with
[func@GObject.signal_emit_by_name] if they need to control
the cursor programmatically.

The default bindings for this signal come in two variants, the
variant with the <kbd>Shift</kbd> modifier extends the selection,
the variant without the <kbd>Shift</kbd> modifier does not.
There are too many key combinations to list them all here.

- <kbd>←</kbd>, <kbd>→</kbd>, <kbd>↑</kbd>, <kbd>↓</kbd>
  move by individual characters/lines
- <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>←</kbd>, etc.

name: GtkLayoutChild
doc: The base class for objects that are meant to hold layout properties.

properties.name: layout-manager
properties.doc: The layout manager that created the `GtkLayoutChild` instance.

name: GtkLayoutManager
doc:
Handles the preferred size and allocation for children of a widget.

You typically subclass `GtkLayoutManager` if you want to implement a
layout policy for the children of a widget, or if you want to determine
the size of a widget depending on its contents.

Each `GtkWidget` can only have a `GtkLayoutManager` instance associated
to it at any given time; it is possible, though, to replace the layout
manager instance using [method@Gtk.Widget.set_layout_manager].

## Layout properties

A layout manager can expose properties for controlling the layout of
each child, by creating an object type derived from [class@Gtk.LayoutChild]
and installing the properties on it as normal `GObject` properties.

Each `GtkLayoutChild` instance storing the layout properties for a
specific child is created through the [method@Gtk.LayoutManager.get_layout_child]
method; a `GtkLayoutManager` controls the creation of its `GtkLayoutChild`
instances by overriding the GtkLayoutManagerClass.create_layout_child()
virtual function.

name: GtkListBase
doc:
The abstract base class for GTK's list widgets.

# Shortcuts and Gestures

`GtkListBase` supports the following keyboard shortcuts:

- <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>A</kbd> or <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>/</kbd>
  selects all items.
- <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>Shift</kbd>+<kbd>A</kbd> or
  <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>\</kbd> unselects all items.

The focused item is controlled by the navigation keys below, combined
with the <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> modifier to prevent moving the selection,
and the <kbd>Shift</kbd> modifier to extend the current selection.

- <kbd>←</kbd>, <kbd>→</kbd>, <kbd>↑</kbd>, <kbd>↓</kbd> move the focus
  on the next item in the designed direction.
- <kbd>Home</kbd> and <kbd>End</kbd> focus the first or last item.
- <kbd>PgUp</kbd> and <kbd>PgDn</kbd> move the focus one page up or down.

List item widgets support the following keyboard shortcuts:

- <kbd>Enter</kbd> activates the item.
- <kbd>␣</kbd> selects the item, with the same <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> and
  <kbd>Shift</kbd> modifiers combinations as the navigation keys.

# Actions

`GtkListBase` defines a set of built-in actions:

- `list.scroll-to-item` moves the visible area to the item at given position
  with the minimum amount of scrolling required.

name: GtkListItem
doc:
Used by list widgets to represent items in a [iface@Gio.ListModel].

`GtkListItem` objects are managed by the list widget (with its factory)
and cannot be created by applications, but they need to be populated
by application code.

name: GtkListItemFactory
doc:
Creates widgets for the items taken from a `GListModel`.

This is one of the core concepts of handling list widgets such
as [class@Gtk.ListView] or [class@Gtk.GridView].

The `GtkListItemFactory` is tasked with creating widgets for items
taken from the model when the views need them and updating them as
the items displayed by the view change.

A view is usually only able to display anything after both a factory
and a model have been set on the view.

name: GtkListStore
doc:
A list-like data structure that can be used with the [class@Gtk.TreeView].

The `GtkListStore` object is a list model for use with a `GtkTreeView`
widget.

name: GtkListView
doc:
Presents a large dynamic list of items.

`GtkListView` uses its factory to generate one row widget for each visible
item and shows them in a linear display, either vertically or horizontally.

The [property@Gtk.ListView:show-separators] property offers a simple way to
display separators between the rows.

`GtkListView` allows the user to select items according to the selection
characteristics of the model.

name: GtkLockButton
doc: `GtkLockButton` is a widget to obtain and revoke authorizations needed to operate the controls. It is typically used in preference dialogs or control panels. The required authorization is represented by a `GPermission` object. Concrete implementations of `GPermission` may use PolicyKit or some other authorization framework.

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

name: GtkMediaFile
doc: Implements the `GtkMediaStream` interface for files.

properties.name: file
properties.doc: The file being played back or %NULL if not playing a file.

properties.name: input-stream
properties.doc: The stream being played back or %NULL if not playing a stream.

properties.name: duration
properties.doc: The stream's duration in microseconds or 0 if unknown.

properties.name: ended
properties.doc: Set when playback has finished.

properties.name: error
properties.doc:
%NULL for a properly working stream or the `GError`
that the stream is in.

properties.name: has-audio
properties.doc: Whether the stream contains audio.

properties.name: has-video
properties.doc: Whether the stream contains video.

properties.name: loop
properties.doc: Try to restart the media from the beginning once it ended.

properties.name: muted
properties.doc: Whether the audio stream should be muted.

properties.name: playing
properties.doc: Whether the stream is currently playing.

properties.name: prepared
properties.doc:
Whether the stream has finished initializing and existence of
audio and video is known.

properties.name: seekable
properties.doc: Set unless the stream is known to not support seeking.

properties.name: seeking
properties.doc: Set while a seek is in progress.

properties.name: timestamp
properties.doc: The current presentation timestamp in microseconds.

properties.name: volume
properties.doc: Volume of the audio stream.

name: GtkMenuButton
doc: Displays a popup when clicked. This popup can be provided either as a `GtkPopover` or as an abstract `GMenuModel`. The `GtkMenuButton` widget can show either an icon (set with the [property@Gtk.MenuButton:icon-name] property) or a label (set with the [property@Gtk.MenuButton:label] property).

properties.name: active
properties.doc: Whether the menu button is active.

properties.name: always-show-arrow
properties.doc: Whether to show a dropdown arrow even when using an icon or a custom child.

properties.name: can-shrink
properties.doc:
Whether the size of the button can be made smaller than the natural
size of its contents.

properties.name: child
properties.doc: The child widget.

properties.name: direction
properties.doc:
The `GtkArrowType` representing the direction in which the
menu or popover will be popped out.

properties.name: has-frame
properties.doc: Whether the button has a frame.

properties.name: icon-name
properties.doc: The name of the icon used to automatically populate the button.

properties.name: label
properties.doc: The label for the button.

properties.name: menu-model
properties.doc: The `GMenuModel` from which the popup will be created.

properties.name: popover
properties.doc: The `GtkPopover` that will be popped up when the button is clicked.

properties.name: primary
properties.doc: Whether the menu button acts as a primary menu.

properties.name: use-underline
properties.doc: If set an underscore in the text indicates a mnemonic.

properties.name: can-focus
properties.doc:
Whether the widget or any of its descendents can accept
the input focus.

properties.name: can-target
properties.doc: Whether the widget can receive pointer events.

properties.name: css-classes
properties.doc: A list of css classes applied to this widget.

properties.name: css-name
properties.doc: The name of this widget in the CSS tree.

properties.name: cursor
properties.doc: The cursor used by @widget.

properties.name: focus-on-click
properties.doc: Whether the widget should grab focus when it is clicked with the mouse.

properties.name: focusable
properties.doc: Whether this widget itself will accept the input focus.

properties.name: halign
properties.doc: How to distribute horizontal space if widget gets extra space.

properties.name: has-default
properties.doc: Whether the widget is the default widget.

properties.name: has-focus
properties.doc: Whether the widget has the input focus.

properties.name: has-tooltip
properties.doc:
Enables or disables the emission of the [signal@Gtk.Widget::query-tooltip]
signal on @widget.

properties.name: height-request
properties.doc: Overrides for height request of the widget.

properties.name: hexpand
properties.doc: Whether to expand horizontally.

properties.name: hexpand-set
properties.doc: Whether to use the `hexpand` property.

properties.name: layout-manager
properties.doc:
The [class@Gtk.LayoutManager] instance to use to compute
the preferred size of the widget, and allocate its children.

properties.name: limit-events
properties.doc:
Makes this widget act like a modal dialog, with respect to
event delivery.

Global event controllers will not handle events with targets
inside the widget, unless they are set up to ignore propagation
limits.

properties.doc: Margin on bottom side of widget.

properties.name: margin-end
properties.doc: Margin on end of widget, horizontally.

properties.name: margin-start
properties.doc: Margin on start of widget, horizontally.

properties.name: margin-top
properties.doc: Margin on top side of widget.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the widget.

properties.name: opacity
properties.doc: The requested opacity of the widget.

properties.name: overflow
properties.doc: How content outside the widget's content area is treated.

properties.name: parent
properties.doc: The parent widget of this widget.

properties.name: receives-default
properties.doc: Whether the widget will receive the default action when it is focused.

properties.name: root
properties.doc: The `GtkRoot` widget of the widget tree containing this widget.

properties.name: scale-factor
properties.doc: The scale factor of the widget.

properties.name: sensitive
properties.doc: Whether the widget responds to input.

properties.name: tooltip-markup

properties.doc: How to distribute vertical space if widget gets extra space.

properties.name: width-request

name: GtkMessageDialog
doc: `GtkMessageDialog` presents a dialog with some message text. It’s simply a convenience widget; you could construct the equivalent of `GtkMessageDialog` from `GtkDialog` without too much effort, but `GtkMessageDialog` saves typing. The easiest way to do a modal message dialog is to use the %GTK_DIALOG_MODAL flag, which will call [method@Gtk.Window.set_modal] internally.

properties.name: buttons
properties.doc: Set of buttons to display on the dialog.

properties.name: message-area
properties.doc: The `GtkBox` that corresponds to the message area of this dialog.

properties.name: message-type
properties.doc: The type of the message.

properties.name: secondary-text
properties.doc: The secondary text of the message dialog.

properties.name: secondary-use-markup
properties.doc: %TRUE if the secondary text of the dialog includes Pango markup.

properties.name: text
properties.doc: The primary text of the message dialog.

properties.name: use-markup
properties.doc: %TRUE if the primary text of the dialog includes Pango markup.

properties.name: use-header-bar
properties.doc:
%TRUE if the dialog uses a headerbar for action buttons
instead of the action-area.

properties.name: application
properties.doc: The `GtkApplication` associated with the window.

properties.doc: The display that will display this window.

properties.doc: Whether 'focus rectangles' are currently visible in this window.

properties.name: focus-widget
properties.doc: The focus widget.

properties.doc: The gravity to use when resizing the window programmatically.

properties.name: handle-menubar-accel

properties.name: hide-on-close

properties.name: icon-name

name: GtkMnemonicAction
doc: Activates a widget with a mnemonic.

doc: Triggers when a specific mnemonic is pressed.

properties.doc: The key value for the trigger.

name: GtkMountOperation
doc:
Asks the user for passwords and other information required to
mount a volume.

properties.name: display
properties.doc: The display where dialogs will be shown.

properties.name: is-showing
properties.doc: Whether a dialog is currently shown.

properties.name: parent

name: GtkMultiFilter
doc: Base class for filters that combine multiple filters.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the filter changed.

Users of the filter should then check items again via
[method@Gtk.Filter.match].

`GtkFilterListModel` handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, not all items need
to be checked, but only some.

name: GtkMultiSelection
doc: A selection model that allows selecting multiple elements.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: model
properties.doc: The list managed by this selection.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

name: GtkMultiSorter
doc: Combines multiple sorters by trying them in turn.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the sorter changed.

Users of the sorter should then update the sort order
again via gtk_sorter_compare().

[class@Gtk.SortListModel] handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, it may be possible to update
the sort order without a full resorting.

name: GtkNamedAction
doc: Activates a named action.

properties.name: action-name

name: GtkNativeDialog
doc:
Base class for platform dialogs that don't use `GtkDialog`.

Native dialogs are used in order to integrate better with a platform,
by looking the same as other native applications and supporting
platform specific features.

The [class@Gtk.Dialog] functions cannot be used on such objects,
but we need a similar API in order to drive them.

properties.name: modal
properties.doc: Whether the window should be modal with respect to its transient parent.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: The title of the dialog window

properties.name: transient-for
properties.doc: The transient parent of the dialog, or %NULL for none.

properties.name: visible
properties.doc: Whether the window is currently visible.

signals.doc: Emitted when the user responds to the dialog.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

Emitted whenever the sorter changed.

Users of the sorter should then update the sort order
again via gtk_sorter_compare().

[class@Gtk.SortListModel] handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, it may be possible to update
the sort order without a full resorting.

signals.name: get-child-position
signals.doc:
Emitted to determine the position and size of any overlay
child widgets.

A handler for this signal should fill @allocation with
the desired position and size for @widget, relative to
the 'main' child of @overlay.

The default handler for this signal uses the @widget's
halign and valign properties to determine the position
and gives the widget its natural size (except that an
alignment of %GTK_ALIGN_FILL will cause the overlay to
be full-width/height).

name: GtkOverlayLayout
doc:
The layout manager used by [class@Gtk.Overlay].

It places widgets as overlays on top of the main child.

This is not a reusable layout manager, since it expects its widget
to be a `GtkOverlay`.

name: GtkPadController
doc:
Handles input from the pads found in drawing tablets.

Pads are the collection of buttons and tactile sensors often found around
the stylus-sensitive area.

These buttons and sensors have no implicit meaning, and by default they
perform no action.

properties.name: action-group
properties.doc: The action group of the controller.

properties.doc: The pad of the controller.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name for this controller, typically used for debugging purposes.

properties.name: propagation-limit
properties.doc: The limit for which events this controller will handle.

properties.name: propagation-phase
properties.doc: The propagation phase at which this controller will handle events.

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The widget receiving the `GdkEvents` that the controller will handle.

name: GtkPageSetup
doc:
Stores page size, orientation and margins for printing.

The idea is that you can get one of these from the page setup dialog
and then pass it to the `GtkPrintOperation` when printing.
The benefit of splitting this out of the `GtkPrintSettings` is that
these affect the actual layout of the page, and thus need to be set
long before user prints.

## Margins

The margins specified in this object are the “print margins”, i.e.

name: GtkPaned
doc: Arranges its children in two panes, horizontally or vertically. The division between the two panes is adjustable by the user by dragging a handle. Child widgets are added to the panes of the widget with [method@Gtk.Paned.set_start_child] and [method@Gtk.Paned.set_end_child]. The division between the two children is set by default from the size requests of the children, but it can be adjusted by the user. A paned widget draws a separator between the two child widgets and a small handle that the user can drag to adjust the division.

properties.name: end-child
properties.doc: The second child.

properties.doc:

properties.doc: Position of the separator in pixels, from the left/top.

properties.doc: Whether the [property@Gtk.Paned:position] property has been set.

A `GtkEntryBuffer` that locks the underlying memory to prevent it
from being swapped to disk.

signals.name: inserted-text

name: GtkPicture
doc: Displays a `GdkPaintable`. Many convenience functions are provided to make pictures simple to use. For example, if you want to load an image from a file, and then display it, there’s a convenience function to do this: ```c GtkWidget *widget = gtk_picture_new_for_filename ("myfile.png"); ``` If the file isn’t loaded successfully, the picture will contain a “broken image” icon similar to that used in many web browsers. If you want to handle errors in loading the file yourself, for example by displaying an error message, then load the image with and image loading framework such as libglycin, then create the `GtkPicture` with [ctor@Gtk.Picture.new_for_paintable]. Sometimes an application will want to avoid depending on external data files, such as image files.

properties.name: alternative-text
properties.doc: The alternative textual description for the picture.

properties.name: can-shrink
properties.doc: If the `GtkPicture` can be made smaller than the natural size of its contents.

properties.name: content-fit
properties.doc: How the content should be resized to fit inside the `GtkPicture`.

properties.name: file
properties.doc: The `GFile` that is displayed or %NULL if none.

properties.name: keep-aspect-ratio
properties.doc:
Whether the GtkPicture will render its contents trying to preserve the aspect
ratio.

properties.name: paintable
properties.doc: The `GdkPaintable` to be displayed by this `GtkPicture`.

name: GtkPrintContext
doc:
Encapsulates context information that is required when
drawing pages for printing.

This includes the cairo context and important parameters like page size
and resolution.

name: GtkPrintDialog
doc:
Asynchronous API to present a print dialog to the user.

`GtkPrintDialog` collects the arguments that are needed to present
 the dialog, such as a title for the dialog and whether it should
 be modal.

The dialog is shown with the [method@Gtk.PrintDialog.setup] function.

The actual printing can be done with [method@Gtk.PrintDialog.print] or
[method@Gtk.PrintDialog.print_file].

properties.name: accept-label
properties.doc:
A label that may be shown on the accept button of a print dialog
that is presented by [method@Gtk.PrintDialog.setup].

properties.name: modal
properties.doc: Whether the print dialog is modal.

properties.name: page-setup
properties.doc: The page setup to use.

properties.name: print-settings
properties.doc: The print settings to use.

properties.name: title
properties.doc:
A title that may be shown on the print dialog that is
presented by [method@Gtk.PrintDialog.setup].

name: GtkPrintJob
doc:
Represents a job that is sent to a printer.

You only need to deal directly with print jobs if you use the
non-portable [class@Gtk.PrintUnixDialog] API.

Use [method@Gtk.PrintJob.get_surface] to obtain the cairo surface
onto which the pages must be drawn.

properties.name: page-setup
properties.doc: Page setup.

properties.name: printer
properties.doc: The printer to send the job to.

properties.name: settings
properties.doc: Printer settings.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: The title of the print job.

properties.name: track-print-status
properties.doc:
%TRUE if the print job will continue to emit status-changed
signals after the print data has been setn to the printer.

signals.name: status-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the status of a job changes.

name: GtkPrintOperation
doc:
High-level, portable printing API.

It looks a bit different than other GTK dialogs such as the
`GtkFileChooser`, since some platforms don’t expose enough
infrastructure to implement a good print dialog.

properties.name: allow-async
properties.doc:
Determines whether the print operation may run asynchronously or not.

Some systems don't support asynchronous printing, but those that do
will return %GTK_PRINT_OPERATION_RESULT_IN_PROGRESS as the status, and
emit the [signal@Gtk.PrintOperation::done] signal when the operation
is actually done.

The Windows port does not support asynchronous operation at all (this
is unlikely to change).

properties.name: current-page
properties.doc: The current page in the document.

properties.name: custom-tab-label
properties.doc: Used as the label of the tab containing custom widgets.

properties.name: default-page-setup
properties.doc: The `GtkPageSetup` used by default.

properties.name: embed-page-setup
properties.doc:
If %TRUE, page size combo box and orientation combo box
are embedded into page setup page.

properties.name: export-filename
properties.doc: The name of a file to generate instead of showing the print dialog.

properties.name: has-selection
properties.doc: Determines whether there is a selection in your application.

properties.name: job-name
properties.doc: A string used to identify the job (e.g.

properties.name: n-pages
properties.doc:
The number of pages in the document.

This must be set to a positive number before the rendering
starts.

properties.name: n-pages-to-print
properties.doc: The number of pages that will be printed.

properties.name: print-settings
properties.doc: The `GtkPrintSettings` used for initializing the dialog.

properties.name: show-progress
properties.doc:
Determines whether to show a progress dialog during the
print operation.

properties.name: status
properties.doc: The status of the print operation.

properties.name: status-string
properties.doc:
A string representation of the status of the print operation.

The string is translated and suitable for displaying the print
status e.g.

properties.name: support-selection
properties.doc: If %TRUE, the print operation will support print of selection.

properties.name: track-print-status
properties.doc:
If %TRUE, the print operation will try to continue report on
the status of the print job in the printer queues and printer.

properties.name: unit
properties.doc:
The transformation for the cairo context obtained from
`GtkPrintContext` is set up in such a way that distances
are measured in units of @unit.

properties.name: use-full-page
properties.doc:
If %TRUE, the transformation for the cairo context obtained
from `GtkPrintContext` puts the origin at the top left corner
of the page.

This may not be the top left corner of the sheet, depending on
page orientation and the number of pages per sheet.

signals.name: begin-print
signals.doc:
Emitted after the user has finished changing print settings
in the dialog, before the actual rendering starts.

signals.name: create-custom-widget
signals.doc:
Emitted when displaying the print dialog.

If you return a widget in a handler for this signal it will be
added to a custom tab in the print dialog.

signals.name: custom-widget-apply
signals.doc:
Emitted right before ::begin-print if you added
a custom widget in the ::create-custom-widget handler.

signals.name: done
signals.doc:
Emitted when the print operation run has finished doing
everything required for printing.

signals.name: draw-page
signals.doc: Emitted for every page that is printed.

signals.name: end-print
signals.doc: Emitted after all pages have been rendered.

signals.name: paginate
signals.doc:
Emitted after the ::begin-print signal, but before the actual rendering
starts.

It keeps getting emitted until a connected signal handler returns %TRUE.

The ::paginate signal is intended to be used for paginating a document
in small chunks, to avoid blocking the user interface for a long
time.

signals.name: preview
signals.doc:
Gets emitted when a preview is requested from the native dialog.

The default handler for this signal uses an external viewer
application to preview.

To implement a custom print preview, an application must return
%TRUE from its handler for this signal.

signals.name: request-page-setup
signals.doc: Emitted once for every page that is printed.

signals.name: status-changed
signals.doc: Emitted at between the various phases of the print operation.

signals.name: update-custom-widget
signals.doc: Emitted after change of selected printer.

name: GtkPrintSettings
doc:
Collects the settings of a print dialog in a system-independent way.

The main use for this object is that once you’ve printed you can get a
settings object that represents the settings the user chose, and the next
time you print you can pass that object in so that the user doesn’t have
to re-set all his settings.

Its also possible to enumerate the settings so that you can easily save
the settings for the next time your app runs, or even store them in a
document.

name: GtkPrintUnixDialog
doc: A print dialog for platforms which don’t provide a native print dialog, like Unix. It can be used very much like any other GTK dialog, at the cost of the portability offered by the high-level printing API with [class@Gtk.PrintOperation]. In order to print something with `GtkPrintUnixDialog`, you need to use [method@Gtk.PrintUnixDialog.get_selected_printer] to obtain a [class@Gtk.Printer] object and use it to construct a [class@Gtk.PrintJob] using [ctor@Gtk.PrintJob.new]. `GtkPrintUnixDialog` uses the following response values: - %GTK_RESPONSE_OK: for the “Print” button - %GTK_RESPONSE_APPLY: for the “Preview” button - %GTK_RESPONSE_CANCEL: for the “Cancel” button # GtkPrintUnixDialog as GtkBuildable The `GtkPrintUnixDialog` implementation of the `GtkBuildable` interface exposes its @notebook internal children with the name “notebook”. An example of a `GtkPrintUnixDialog` UI definition fragment: ```xml ``` # CSS nodes `GtkPrintUnixDialog` has a single CSS node with name window.

name: GtkPrinter
doc:
Represents a printer.

You only need to deal directly with printers if you use the
non-portable [class@Gtk.PrintUnixDialog] API.

A `GtkPrinter` allows to get status information about the printer,
such as its description, its location, the number of queued jobs,
etc.

The text will be ellipsized if the progress bar does not have enough room

name: GtkRange
doc:
Base class for widgets which visualize an adjustment.

Widgets that are derived from `GtkRange` include
[class@Gtk.Scale] and [class@Gtk.Scrollbar].

Apart from signals for monitoring the parameters of the adjustment,
`GtkRange` provides properties and methods for setting a
“fill level” on range widgets.

name: GtkRecentManager
doc:
Manages and looks up recently used files.

Each recently used file is identified by its URI, and has meta-data
associated to it, like the names and command lines of the applications
that have registered it, the number of time each application has
registered the same file, the mime type of the file and whether
the file should be displayed only by the applications that have
registered it.

The recently used files list is per user.

`GtkRecentManager` acts like a database of all the recently
used files.

properties.name: filename
properties.doc:
The full path to the file to be used to store and read the
recently used resources list

properties.name: size
properties.doc: The size of the recently used resources list.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted when the current recently used resources manager changes
its contents.

name: GtkRevealer
doc:
Animates the transition of its child from invisible to visible.

The style of transition can be controlled with
[method@Gtk.Revealer.set_transition_type].

These animations respect the [property@Gtk.Settings:gtk-enable-animations]
setting.

# CSS nodes

`GtkRevealer` has a single CSS node with name revealer.
When styling `GtkRevealer` using CSS, remember that it only hides its contents,
not itself.

name: GtkScale
doc: Allows to select a numeric value with a slider control. To use it, you’ll probably want to investigate the methods on its base class, [class@Gtk.Range], in addition to the methods for `GtkScale` itself. To set the value of a scale, you would normally use [method@Gtk.Range.set_value]. To detect changes to the value, you would normally use the [signal@Gtk.Range::value-changed] signal. Note that using the same upper and lower bounds for the `GtkScale` (through the `GtkRange` methods) will hide the slider itself.

properties.name: digits
properties.doc: The number of decimal places that are displayed in the value.

upper boundary set by the fill level.

name: GtkSearchEntry
doc: A single-line text entry widget for use as a search entry. The main API for interacting with a `GtkSearchEntry` as entry is the `GtkEditable` interface. It will show an inactive symbolic “find” icon when the search entry is empty, and a symbolic “clear” icon when there is text. Clicking on the “clear” icon will empty the search entry. To make filtering appear more reactive, it is a good idea to not react to every change in the entry text immediately, but only after a short delay.

name: GtkSettings
doc:
Provides a mechanism to share global settings between applications.

On the X window system, this sharing is realized by an
[XSettings](http://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Specifications/xsettings-spec)
manager that is usually part of the desktop environment, along with
utilities that let the user change these settings.

On Wayland, the settings are obtained either via a settings portal,
or by reading desktop settings from [class@Gio.Settings].

On macOS, the settings are obtained from `NSUserDefaults`.

In the absence of these sharing mechanisms, GTK reads default values for
settings from `settings.ini` files in `/etc/gtk-4.0`, `$XDG_CONFIG_DIRS/gtk-4.0`
and `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/gtk-4.0`.

properties.name: gtk-alternative-button-order
properties.doc: Whether buttons in dialogs should use the alternative button order.

properties.name: gtk-alternative-sort-arrows
properties.doc:
Controls the direction of the sort indicators in sorted list and tree
views.

By default an arrow pointing down means the column is sorted
in ascending order.

properties.name: gtk-application-prefer-dark-theme
properties.doc:
Whether the application prefers to use a dark theme.

If a GTK theme includes a dark variant, it will be used
instead of the configured theme.

Some applications benefit from minimizing the amount of light
pollution that interferes with the content.

properties.name: gtk-cursor-aspect-ratio
properties.doc: The aspect ratio of the text caret.

properties.name: gtk-cursor-blink
properties.doc: Whether the cursor should blink.

properties.name: gtk-cursor-blink-time
properties.doc: Length of the cursor blink cycle, in milliseconds.

properties.name: gtk-cursor-blink-timeout
properties.doc: Time after which the cursor stops blinking, in seconds.

properties.name: gtk-cursor-theme-name
properties.doc: Name of the cursor theme to use.

properties.name: gtk-cursor-theme-size
properties.doc: The size to use for cursors.

properties.name: gtk-decoration-layout
properties.doc:
Determines which buttons should be put in the
titlebar of client-side decorated windows, and whether they
should be placed on the left or right.

The format of the string is button names, separated by commas.
A colon separates the buttons that should appear on the left
from those on the right.

properties.name: gtk-dialogs-use-header
properties.doc:
Whether builtin GTK dialogs such as the file chooser, the
color chooser or the font chooser will use a header bar at
the top to show action widgets, or an action area at the bottom.

properties.name: gtk-dnd-drag-threshold
properties.doc: The number of pixels the cursor can move before dragging.

properties.name: gtk-double-click-distance
properties.doc:
The maximum distance allowed between two clicks for them to be considered
a double click, in pixels.

properties.name: gtk-double-click-time
properties.doc:
The maximum time to allow between two clicks for them to be considered
a double click, in milliseconds.

properties.name: gtk-enable-accels
properties.doc:
Whether menu items should have visible accelerators which can be
activated.

properties.name: gtk-enable-animations
properties.doc: Whether to enable toolkit-wide animations.

properties.name: gtk-enable-event-sounds
properties.doc: Whether to play any event sounds at all.

properties.name: gtk-enable-input-feedback-sounds
properties.doc: Whether to play event sounds as feedback to user input.

properties.name: gtk-enable-primary-paste
properties.doc:
Whether a middle click on a mouse should paste the
'PRIMARY' clipboard content at the cursor location.

properties.name: gtk-entry-password-hint-timeout
properties.doc:
How long to show the last input character in hidden
entries.

This value is in milliseconds.

properties.name: gtk-entry-select-on-focus
properties.doc: Whether to select the contents of an entry when it is focused.

properties.name: gtk-error-bell
properties.doc:
When %TRUE, keyboard navigation and other input-related errors
will cause a beep.

properties.name: gtk-font-name
properties.doc: The default font to use.

properties.name: gtk-font-rendering
properties.doc: How GTK font rendering is set up.

properties.name: gtk-fontconfig-timestamp
properties.doc: Timestamp of the current fontconfig configuration.

properties.name: gtk-hint-font-metrics
properties.doc: Whether hinting should be applied to font metrics.

properties.name: gtk-icon-theme-name
properties.doc: Name of the icon theme to use.

properties.name: gtk-im-module
properties.doc: Which IM (input method) module should be used by default.

properties.name: gtk-interface-color-scheme
properties.doc: The color scheme used for rendering the user interface.

properties.name: gtk-interface-contrast
properties.doc: The level of contrast to use for the user interface.

properties.name: gtk-keynav-use-caret
properties.doc:
Whether GTK should make sure that text can be navigated with
a caret, even if it is not editable.

properties.name: gtk-label-select-on-focus
properties.doc:
Whether to select the contents of a selectable
label when it is focused.

properties.name: gtk-long-press-time
properties.doc: The time for a button or touch press to be considered a “long press”.

properties.name: gtk-overlay-scrolling
properties.doc: Whether scrolled windows may use overlaid scrolling indicators.

properties.name: gtk-primary-button-warps-slider
properties.doc:
If the value of this setting is %TRUE, clicking the primary button in a
`GtkRange` trough will move the slider, and hence set the range’s value, to
the point that you clicked.

properties.name: gtk-print-backends
properties.doc:
A comma-separated list of print backends to use in the print
dialog.

properties.name: gtk-print-preview-command
properties.doc:
A command to run for displaying the print preview.

The command should contain a `%f` placeholder, which will get
replaced by the path to the pdf file.

properties.name: gtk-recent-files-enabled
properties.doc:
Whether GTK should keep track of items inside the recently used
resources list.

properties.name: gtk-recent-files-max-age
properties.doc:
The maximum age, in days, of the items inside the recently used
resources list.

properties.name: gtk-shell-shows-app-menu
properties.doc:
Set to %TRUE if the desktop environment is displaying
the app menu, %FALSE if the app should display it itself.

properties.name: gtk-shell-shows-desktop
properties.doc:
Set to %TRUE if the desktop environment is displaying
the desktop folder, %FALSE if not.

properties.name: gtk-shell-shows-menubar
properties.doc:
Set to %TRUE if the desktop environment is displaying
the menubar, %FALSE if the app should display it itself.

properties.name: gtk-show-status-shapes
properties.doc: When %TRUE, widgets like switches include shapes to indicate their on/off state.

properties.name: gtk-sound-theme-name
properties.doc: The XDG sound theme to use for event sounds.

properties.name: gtk-split-cursor
properties.doc:
Whether two cursors should be displayed for mixed left-to-right and
right-to-left text.

properties.name: gtk-theme-name
properties.doc: Name of the theme to load.

properties.name: gtk-titlebar-double-click
properties.doc:
Determines the action to take when a double-click
occurs on the titlebar of client-side decorated windows.

properties.name: gtk-titlebar-middle-click
properties.doc:
Determines the action to take when a middle-click
occurs on the titlebar of client-side decorated windows.

properties.name: gtk-titlebar-right-click
properties.doc:
Determines the action to take when a right-click
occurs on the titlebar of client-side decorated windows.

properties.name: gtk-xft-antialias
properties.doc: Whether to antialias fonts.

properties.name: gtk-xft-dpi
properties.doc: The font resolution, in 1024 * dots/inch.

properties.name: gtk-xft-hinting
properties.doc: Whether to enable font hinting.

properties.name: gtk-xft-hintstyle
properties.doc: What degree of font hinting to use.

properties.name: gtk-xft-rgba
properties.doc: The type of subpixel antialiasing to use.

name: GtkShortcut
doc:
Describes a keyboard shortcut.

It contains a description of how to trigger the shortcut via a
[class@Gtk.ShortcutTrigger] and a way to activate the shortcut
on a widget via a [class@Gtk.ShortcutAction].

The actual work is usually done via [class@Gtk.ShortcutController],
which decides if and when to activate a shortcut.

properties.name: action

properties during construction.

name: GtkShortcutsWindow
doc: A `GtkShortcutsWindow` shows information about the keyboard shortcuts and gestures of an application. The shortcuts can be grouped, and you can have multiple sections in this window, corresponding to the major modes of your application. Additionally, the shortcuts can be filtered by the current view, to avoid showing information that is not relevant in the current application context. The recommended way to construct a `GtkShortcutsWindow` is with [class@Gtk.Builder], by using the `` tag to populate a `GtkShortcutsWindow` with one or more [class@Gtk.ShortcutsSection] objects, which contain one or more [class@Gtk.ShortcutsGroup] instances, which, in turn, contain [class@Gtk.ShortcutsShortcut] instances. If you need to add a section programmatically, use [method@Gtk.ShortcutsWindow.add_section] instead of [method@Gtk.Window.set_child], as the shortcuts window manages its children directly. # A simple example: This example has as single section.

properties.name: section-name
properties.doc: The name of the section to show.

properties.name: view-name
properties.doc: The view name by which to filter the contents.

properties.name: application
properties.doc: The `GtkApplication` associated with the window.

properties.name: child
properties.doc: The child widget.

properties.name: decorated
properties.doc: Whether the window should have a frame (also known as *decorations*).

properties.name: default-height
properties.doc: The default height of the window.

properties.name: default-widget
properties.doc: The default widget.

properties.name: default-width
properties.doc: The default width of the window.

properties.name: deletable
properties.doc: Whether the window frame should have a close button.

properties.name: destroy-with-parent
properties.doc: If this window should be destroyed when the parent is destroyed.

properties.name: display
properties.doc: The display that will display this window.

properties.name: focus-visible
properties.doc: Whether 'focus rectangles' are currently visible in this window.

properties.name: focus-widget
properties.doc: The focus widget.

properties.name: fullscreened
properties.doc: Whether the window is fullscreen.

properties.name: gravity
properties.doc: The gravity to use when resizing the window programmatically.

properties.name: handle-menubar-accel
properties.doc:
Whether the window frame should handle <kbd>F10</kbd> for activating
menubars.

properties.name: hide-on-close
properties.doc:
If this window should be hidden instead of destroyed when the user clicks
the close button.

properties.name: icon-name
properties.doc: Specifies the name of the themed icon to use as the window icon.

properties.name: is-active
properties.doc: Whether the toplevel is the currently active window.

properties.name: maximized
properties.doc: Whether the window is maximized.

properties.name: mnemonics-visible
properties.doc: Whether mnemonics are currently visible in this window.

properties.name: modal
properties.doc: If true, the window is modal.

properties.name: resizable
properties.doc: If true, users can resize the window.

properties.name: startup-id
properties.doc: A write-only property for setting window's startup notification identifier.

properties.name: suspended
properties.doc: Whether the window is suspended.

properties.name: title
properties.doc: The title of the window.

properties.name: titlebar
properties.doc: The titlebar widget.

properties.name: transient-for
properties.doc: The transient parent of the window.

properties.name: can-focus
properties.doc:
Whether the widget or any of its descendents can accept
the input focus.

properties.name: can-target

above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.
