name: GtkSignalListItemFactory
doc:
Emits signals to manage listitems.

Signals are emitted for every listitem in the same order:

 1.

signals.name: bind
signals.doc: Emitted when an object has been bound to an item.

signals.name: setup
signals.doc: Emitted when a newly created listitem needs to be prepared for use.

signals.name: teardown
signals.doc: Emitted when an object is about to be destroyed.

signals.name: unbind
signals.doc: Emitted when an object has been unbound from its item.

name: GtkSingleSelection
doc:
A selection model that allows selecting a single item.

Note that the selection is *persistent* -- if the selected item is removed
and re-added in the same [signal@Gio.ListModel::items-changed] emission, it
stays selected.

properties.name: autoselect
properties.doc: If the selection will always select an item.

properties.name: can-unselect
properties.doc: If unselecting the selected item is allowed.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: model
properties.doc: The model being managed.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

properties.name: selected
properties.doc: Position of the selected item.

properties.name: selected-item
properties.doc: The selected item.

name: GtkSizeGroup
doc:
Groups widgets together so they all request the same size.

This is typically useful when you want a column of widgets to have
the same size, but you can’t use a [class@Gtk.Grid] or [class@Gtk.Box].

In detail, the size requested for each widget in a `GtkSizeGroup` is
the maximum of the sizes that would have been requested for each
widget in the size group if they were not in the size group.

properties.name: mode
properties.doc: The direction in which the size group affects requested sizes.

name: GtkSliceListModel
doc: A list model that presents a slice of another model.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: model
properties.doc: Child model to take slice from.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

properties.name: offset
properties.doc: Offset of slice.

properties.name: size
properties.doc: Maximum size of slice.

name: GtkSnapshot
doc:
Assists in creating [class@Gsk.RenderNode]s for widgets.

It functions in a similar way to a cairo context, and maintains a stack
of render nodes and their associated transformations.

The node at the top of the stack is the one that `gtk_snapshot_append_…()`
functions operate on.

name: GtkSortListModel
doc:
A list model that sorts the elements of another model.

The elements are sorted according to a `GtkSorter`.

The model is a stable sort.

properties.name: incremental
properties.doc: If the model should sort items incrementally.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: model
properties.doc: The model being sorted.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

properties.name: pending
properties.doc: Estimate of unsorted items remaining.

properties.name: section-sorter
properties.doc: The section sorter for this model, if one is set.

properties.name: sorter
properties.doc: The sorter for this model.

name: GtkSorter
doc:
Describes sorting criteria for a [class@Gtk.SortListModel].

Its primary user is [class@Gtk.SortListModel]

The model will use a sorter to determine the order in which
its items should appear by calling [method@Gtk.Sorter.compare]
for pairs of items.

Sorters may change their sorting behavior through their lifetime.
In that case, they will emit the [signal@Gtk.Sorter::changed] signal
to notify that the sort order is no longer valid and should be updated
by calling gtk_sorter_compare() again.

GTK provides various pre-made sorter implementations for common sorting
operations.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the sorter changed.

Users of the sorter should then update the sort order
again via gtk_sorter_compare().

[class@Gtk.SortListModel] handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, it may be possible to update
the sort order without a full resorting.

name: GtkStatusbar
doc: A `GtkStatusbar` widget is usually placed along the bottom of an application's main [class@Gtk.Window]. A `GtkStatusBar` may provide a regular commentary of the application's status (as is usually the case in a web browser, for example), or may be used to simply output a message when the status changes, (when an upload is complete in an FTP client, for example). Status bars in GTK maintain a stack of messages.

name: GtkStringFilter
doc:
Determines whether to include items by comparing strings to a fixed search term.

The strings are obtained from the items by evaluating an expression
set with [method@Gtk.StringFilter.set_expression], and they are
compared against a search term set with [method@Gtk.StringFilter.set_search].

`GtkStringFilter` has several different modes of comparison - it
can match the whole string, just a prefix, or any substring.

properties.name: expression
properties.doc:
The expression to evaluate on each item to get a
string to compare with.

properties.name: ignore-case
properties.doc: If matching is case sensitive.

properties.name: match-mode
properties.doc: If exact matches are necessary or if substrings are allowed.

properties.name: search
properties.doc: The search term.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the filter changed.

Users of the filter should then check items again via
[method@Gtk.Filter.match].

`GtkFilterListModel` handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, not all items need
to be checked, but only some.

name: GtkStringList
doc:
A list model that wraps an array of strings.

The objects in the model are of type [class@Gtk.StringObject] and have
a "string" property that can be used inside expressions.

`GtkStringList` is well-suited for any place where you would
typically use a `char*[]`, but need a list model.

## GtkStringList as GtkBuildable

The `GtkStringList` implementation of the `GtkBuildable` interface
supports adding items directly using the `<items>` element and
specifying `<item>` elements for each item.

properties.name: item-type
properties.doc: The type of items.

properties.name: n-items
properties.doc: The number of items.

properties.name: strings
properties.doc: The strings in the model.

name: GtkStringObject
doc: The type of items in a `GtkStringList`.

properties.name: string
properties.doc: The string.

name: GtkStringSorter
doc: Sorts items by comparing strings.

properties.name: collation
properties.doc: The collation method to use for sorting.

properties.name: expression
properties.doc: The expression to evaluate on item to get a string to compare with.

properties.name: ignore-case
properties.doc: If sorting is case sensitive.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the sorter changed.

Users of the sorter should then update the sort order
again via gtk_sorter_compare().

[class@Gtk.SortListModel] handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, it may be possible to update
the sort order without a full resorting.

name: GtkStyleContext
doc:
`GtkStyleContext` stores styling information affecting a widget.

In order to construct the final style information, `GtkStyleContext`
queries information from all attached `GtkStyleProviders`.

properties.name: display
properties.doc: The display of the style context.

name: GtkText
doc:
A single-line text entry.

`GtkText` is the common implementation of single-line text editing
that is shared between [class@Gtk.Entry], [class@Gtk.PasswordEntry],
[class@Gtk.SpinButton], and other widgets.

properties.name: activates-default
properties.doc: Whether to activate the default widget when <kbd>Enter</kbd> is pressed.

properties.name: attributes
properties.doc:
A list of Pango attributes to apply to the text.

This is mainly useful to change the size or weight of the text.

The `PangoAttribute`'s @start_index and @end_index must refer to the
`GtkEntryBuffer` text, i.e.

properties.name: buffer
properties.doc: The `GtkEntryBuffer` object which stores the text.

properties.name: enable-emoji-completion
properties.doc: Whether to suggest Emoji replacements.

properties.name: extra-menu
properties.doc: A menu model whose contents will be appended to the context menu.

properties.name: im-module
properties.doc:
Which input method module should be used.

See [class@Gtk.IMMulticontext].

Setting this to a non-`NULL` value overrides the system-wide
input method.

properties.name: input-hints
properties.doc:
Additional hints that allow input methods to fine-tune
their behaviour.

properties.name: input-purpose
properties.doc: The purpose of this text field.

properties.name: invisible-char
properties.doc: The character to used when masking contents (in “password mode”).

properties.name: invisible-char-set

properties.name: max-length
properties.doc: Maximum number of characters that are allowed.

properties.name: overwrite-mode
properties.doc: If text is overwritten when typing.

properties.name: placeholder-text
properties.doc:
The text that will be displayed in the `GtkText` when it is empty

properties.name: propagate-text-width
properties.doc: Whether the widget should grow and shrink with the content.

properties.name: scroll-offset
properties.doc: Number of pixels scrolled of the screen to the left.

properties.name: tabs
properties.doc: Custom tabs for this text widget.

properties.name: truncate-multiline
properties.doc: When true, pasted multi-line text is truncated to the first line.

properties.name: visibility
properties.doc: If false, the text is masked with the “invisible char”.

properties.doc: Whether to use the `vexpand` property.

properties.name: visible
properties.doc: Whether the widget is visible.

properties.name: width-request
properties.doc: Overrides for width request of the widget.

signals.name: activate
signals.doc: Emitted when the user hits the <kbd>Enter</kbd> key.

signals.name: backspace
signals.doc: Emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: copy-clipboard
signals.doc: Emitted to copy the selection to the clipboard.

signals.name: cut-clipboard
signals.doc: Emitted to cut the selection to the clipboard.

signals.name: delete-from-cursor
signals.doc: Emitted when the user initiates a text deletion.

signals.name: insert-at-cursor
signals.doc:
Emitted when the user initiates the insertion of a
fixed string at the cursor.

signals.name: insert-emoji
signals.doc: Emitted to present the Emoji chooser.

signals.name: move-cursor
signals.doc:
Emitted when the user initiates a cursor movement.

If the cursor is not visible in @self, this signal causes
the viewport to be moved instead.

This is a [keybinding signal](class.SignalAction.html).

Applications should not connect to it, but may emit it with
[func@GObject.signal_emit_by_name] if they need to control
the cursor programmatically.

The default bindings for this signal come in two variants,
the variant with the <kbd>Shift</kbd> modifier extends the
selection, the variant without it does not.
There are too many key combinations to list them all here.

- <kbd>←</kbd>, <kbd>→</kbd>, <kbd>↑</kbd>, <kbd>↓</kbd>
  move by individual characters/lines
- <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>←</kbd>, etc.

signals.name: paste-clipboard
signals.doc: Emitted to paste the contents of the clipboard.

signals.name: preedit-changed
signals.doc:
Emitted when the preedit text changes.

If an input method is used, the typed text will not immediately
be committed to the buffer.

signals.name: toggle-overwrite
signals.doc: Emitted to toggle the overwrite mode.

signals.name: destroy
signals.doc:
Signals that all holders of a reference to the widget should release
the reference that they hold.

signals.name: direction-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the text direction of a widget changes.

signals.name: hide
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is hidden.

signals.name: keynav-failed
signals.doc: Emitted if keyboard navigation fails.

signals.name: map
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is going to be mapped.

signals.name: mnemonic-activate
signals.doc: Emitted when a widget is activated via a mnemonic.

signals.name: move-focus
signals.doc: Emitted when the focus is moved.

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

signals.name: realize
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is associated with a `GdkSurface`.

signals.name: show
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is shown.

signals.name: state-flags-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the widget state changes.

signals.name: unmap
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is going to be unmapped.

signals.name: unrealize
signals.doc: Emitted when the `GdkSurface` associated with @widget is destroyed.

name: GtkTextBuffer
doc: Stores text and attributes for display in a `GtkTextView`.

properties.name: can-redo
properties.doc: Denotes that the buffer can reapply the last undone action.

properties.name: can-undo
properties.doc: Denotes that the buffer can undo the last applied action.

properties.name: cursor-position
properties.doc: The position of the insert mark.

properties.name: enable-undo
properties.doc: Denotes if support for undoing and redoing changes to the buffer is allowed.

properties.name: has-selection
properties.doc: Whether the buffer has some text currently selected.

properties.name: tag-table
properties.doc: The GtkTextTagTable for the buffer.

properties.name: text
properties.doc: The text content of the buffer.

signals.name: apply-tag
signals.doc: Emitted to apply a tag to a range of text in a `GtkTextBuffer`.

signals.name: begin-user-action
signals.doc:
Emitted at the beginning of a single user-visible
operation on a `GtkTextBuffer`.

signals.name: changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the content of a `GtkTextBuffer` has changed.

signals.name: delete-range
signals.doc:
Emitted to delete a range from a `GtkTextBuffer`.

Note that if your handler runs before the default handler
it must not invalidate the @start and @end iters (or has
to revalidate them).

signals.name: end-user-action
signals.doc:
Emitted at the end of a single user-visible
operation on the `GtkTextBuffer`.

signals.name: insert-child-anchor
signals.doc:
Emitted to insert a `GtkTextChildAnchor` in a `GtkTextBuffer`.

Insertion actually occurs in the default handler.

Note that if your handler runs before the default handler
it must not invalidate the @location iter (or has to
revalidate it).

signals.name: insert-paintable
signals.doc:
Emitted to insert a `GdkPaintable` in a `GtkTextBuffer`.

Insertion actually occurs in the default handler.

Note that if your handler runs before the default handler
it must not invalidate the @location iter (or has to
revalidate it).

signals.name: insert-text
signals.doc:
Emitted to insert text in a `GtkTextBuffer`.

Insertion actually occurs in the default handler.

Note that if your handler runs before the default handler
it must not invalidate the @location iter (or has to
revalidate it).

signals.name: mark-deleted
signals.doc: Emitted as notification after a `GtkTextMark` is deleted.

signals.name: mark-set
signals.doc: Emitted as notification after a `GtkTextMark` is set.

signals.name: modified-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the modified bit of a `GtkTextBuffer` flips.

signals.name: paste-done
signals.doc:
Emitted after paste operation has been completed.

This is useful to properly scroll the view to the end
of the pasted text.

signals.name: redo
signals.doc:
Emitted when a request has been made to redo the
previously undone operation.

signals.name: remove-tag
signals.doc:
Emitted to remove all occurrences of @tag from a range
of text in a `GtkTextBuffer`.

signals.name: undo
signals.doc:
Emitted when a request has been made to undo the
previous operation or set of operations that have
been grouped together.

name: GtkTextChildAnchor
doc: Marks a spot in a `GtkTextBuffer` where child widgets can be “anchored”.

name: GtkTextMark
doc:
Marks a position in a `GtkTextbuffer` that is preserved
across modifications.

You may wish to begin by reading the
[text widget conceptual overview](section-text-widget.html),
which gives an overview of all the objects and data types
related to the text widget and how they work together.

A `GtkTextMark` is like a bookmark in a text buffer; it preserves
a position in the text.

properties.name: left-gravity
properties.doc: Whether the mark has left gravity.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the mark or %NULL if the mark is anonymous.

name: GtkTextTag
doc:
Can be applied to text contained in a `GtkTextBuffer`.

You may wish to begin by reading the
[text widget conceptual overview](section-text-widget.html),
which gives an overview of all the objects and data types
related to the text widget and how they work together.

Tags should be in the [class@Gtk.TextTagTable] for a given
`GtkTextBuffer` before using them with that buffer.

[method@Gtk.TextBuffer.create_tag] is the best way to create tags.
See “gtk4-demo” for numerous examples.

For each property of `GtkTextTag`, there is a “set” property, e.g.
“font-set” corresponds to “font”.

properties.name: accumulative-margin
properties.doc:
Whether the margins accumulate or override each other.

When set to %TRUE the margins of this tag are added to the margins
of any other non-accumulative margins present.

properties.name: allow-breaks
properties.doc: Whether breaks are allowed.

properties.name: allow-breaks-set
properties.doc: Whether the `allow-breaks` property is set.

properties.name: background
properties.doc: Background color as a string.

properties.name: background-full-height
properties.doc:
Whether the background color fills the entire line height
or only the height of the tagged characters.

properties.name: background-full-height-set
properties.doc: Whether the `background-full-height` property is set.

properties.name: background-rgba
properties.doc: Background color as a `GdkRGBA`.

properties.name: background-set
properties.doc: Whether the `background` property is set.

properties.name: direction
properties.doc: Text direction, e.g.

properties.doc: Whether the text can be modified by the user.

properties.name: editable-set
properties.doc: Whether the `editable` property is set.

properties.name: fallback
properties.doc: Whether font fallback is enabled.

properties.name: fallback-set
properties.doc: Whether the `fallback` property is set.

properties.name: family
properties.doc: Name of the font family, e.g.

properties.doc: Whether the `family` property is set.

properties.name: font
properties.doc: Font description as string, e.g.

properties.name: font-desc

properties.name: font-features
properties.doc: OpenType font features, as a string.

properties.name: font-features-set
properties.doc: Whether the `font-features` property is set.

properties.name: foreground
properties.doc: Foreground color as a string.

properties.name: foreground-rgba
properties.doc: Foreground color as a `GdkRGBA`.

properties.name: foreground-set
properties.doc: Whether the `foreground` property is set.

properties.name: indent
properties.doc: Amount to indent the paragraph, in pixels.

properties.name: indent-set
properties.doc: Whether the `indent` property is set.

properties.name: insert-hyphens

properties.doc: Whether the `insert-hyphens` property is set.

properties.name: invisible

properties.name: invisible-set
properties.doc: Whether the `invisible` property is set.

properties.name: justification
properties.doc: Left, right, or center justification.

properties.name: justification-set
properties.doc: Whether the `justification` property is set.

properties.name: language
properties.doc: The language this text is in, as an ISO code.

properties.name: language-set
properties.doc: Whether the `language` property is set.

properties.name: left-margin
properties.doc: Width of the left margin in pixels.

properties.name: left-margin-set
properties.doc: Whether the `left-margin` property is set.

properties.name: letter-spacing
properties.doc: Extra spacing between graphemes, in Pango units.

properties.name: letter-spacing-set
properties.doc: Whether the `letter-spacing` property is set.

properties.name: line-height
properties.doc: Factor to scale line height by.

properties.name: line-height-set
properties.doc: Whether the `line-height` property is set.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name used to refer to the tag.

properties.name: overline
properties.doc: Style of overline for this text.

properties.name: overline-rgba
properties.doc: This property modifies the color of overlines.

properties.name: overline-rgba-set
properties.doc: Whether the `overline-rgba` property is set.

properties.name: overline-set
properties.doc: Whether the `overline` property is set.

properties.name: paragraph-background
properties.doc: The paragraph background color as a string.

properties.name: paragraph-background-rgba
properties.doc: The paragraph background color as a `GdkRGBA`.

properties.name: paragraph-background-set
properties.doc: Whether the `paragraph-background` property is set.

properties.name: pixels-above-lines
properties.doc: Pixels of blank space above paragraphs.

properties.name: pixels-above-lines-set
properties.doc: Whether the `pixels-above-lines` property is set.

properties.name: pixels-below-lines
properties.doc: Pixels of blank space below paragraphs.

properties.name: pixels-below-lines-set
properties.doc: Whether the `pixels-below-lines` property is set.

properties.name: pixels-inside-wrap

properties.name: pixels-inside-wrap-set
properties.doc: Whether the `pixels-inside-wrap` property is set.

properties.name: right-margin
properties.doc: Width of the right margin, in pixels.

properties.name: right-margin-set
properties.doc: Whether the `right-margin` property is set.

properties.name: rise

properties.name: rise-set
properties.doc: Whether the `rise` property is set.

properties.name: scale
properties.doc:
Font size as a scale factor relative to the default font size.

This properly adapts to theme changes, etc.

properties.name: scale-set
properties.doc: Whether the `scale` property is set.

properties.name: sentence
properties.doc: Whether this tag represents a single sentence.

properties.name: sentence-set
properties.doc: Whether the `sentence` property is set.

properties.name: show-spaces
properties.doc: How to render invisible characters.

properties.name: show-spaces-set
properties.doc: Whether the `show-spaces` property is set.

properties.name: size
properties.doc: Font size in Pango units.

properties.name: size-points
properties.doc: Font size in points.

properties.name: size-set
properties.doc: Whether the `size` property is set.

properties.name: stretch
properties.doc: Font stretch as a `PangoStretch`, e.g.

properties.doc: Whether the `stretch` property is set.

properties.name: strikethrough

properties.name: strikethrough-rgba
properties.doc: This property modifies the color of strikeouts.

properties.name: strikethrough-rgba-set

properties.doc: Whether the `strikethrough` property is set.

properties.name: style
properties.doc: Font style as a `PangoStyle`, e.g.

properties.doc: Whether the `style` property is set.

properties.name: tabs

properties.name: text-transform

properties.name: underline

properties.name: underline-rgba
properties.doc:
This property modifies the color of underlines.

If not set, underlines will use the foreground color.

If [property@Gtk.TextTag:underline] is set to %PANGO_UNDERLINE_ERROR,
an alternate color may be applied instead of the foreground.

properties.name: underline-rgba-set
properties.doc: If the `underline-rgba` property has been set.

properties.name: underline-set
properties.doc: Whether the `underline` property is set.

properties.name: variant

properties.doc: Whether the `variant` property is set.

properties.name: weight

properties.name: weight-set
properties.doc: Whether the `weight` property is set.

properties.name: word
properties.doc: Whether this tag represents a single word.

properties.name: word-set
properties.doc: Whether the `word` property is set.

properties.name: wrap-mode
properties.doc:
Whether to wrap lines never, at word boundaries, or
at character boundaries.

properties.name: wrap-mode-set
properties.doc: Whether the `wrap-mode` property is set.

name: GtkTextTagTable
doc: Collects the tags in a `GtkTextBuffer`.

signals.name: tag-added
signals.doc: Emitted every time a new tag is added in the `GtkTextTagTable`.

signals.name: tag-changed
signals.doc: Emitted every time a tag in the `GtkTextTagTable` changes.

signals.name: tag-removed
signals.doc: Emitted every time a tag is removed from the `GtkTextTagTable`.

name: GtkTextView
doc: Displays the contents of a [class@Gtk.TextBuffer]. You may wish to begin by reading the [conceptual overview](section-text-widget.html), which gives an overview of all the objects and data types related to the text widget and how they work together. ## Shortcuts and Gestures `GtkTextView` supports the following keyboard shortcuts: - Shift+F10 or Menu opens the context menu. - Ctrl+Z undoes the last modification. - Ctrl+Y or Ctrl+Shift+Z redoes the last undone modification. - Clear clears the content. Additionally, the following signals have default keybindings: - [signal@Gtk.TextView::backspace] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::copy-clipboard] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::cut-clipboard] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::delete-from-cursor] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::insert-emoji] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::move-cursor] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::paste-clipboard] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::select-all] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::toggle-cursor-visible] - [signal@Gtk.TextView::toggle-overwrite] ## Actions `GtkTextView` defines a set of built-in actions: - `clipboard.copy` copies the contents to the clipboard. - `clipboard.cut` copies the contents to the clipboard and deletes it from the widget. - `clipboard.paste` inserts the contents of the clipboard into the widget. - `menu.popup` opens the context menu. - `misc.insert-emoji` opens the Emoji chooser. - `selection.delete` deletes the current selection. - `selection.select-all` selects all of the widgets content. - `text.redo` redoes the last change to the contents. - `text.undo` undoes the last change to the contents. - `text.clear` clears the content. ## CSS nodes ``` textview.view ├── border.top ├── border.left ├── text │ ╰── [selection] ├── border.right ├── border.bottom ╰── [window.popup] ``` `GtkTextView` has a main css node with name textview and style class .view, and subnodes for each of the border windows, and the main text area, with names border and text, respectively.

properties.name: accepts-tab
properties.doc: Whether Tab will result in a tab character being entered.

properties.name: bottom-margin
properties.doc:
The bottom margin for text in the text view.

Note that this property is confusingly named.

properties.name: buffer
properties.doc: The buffer which is displayed.

properties.name: cursor-visible
properties.doc: If the insertion cursor is shown.

properties.name: editable
properties.doc: Whether the text can be modified by the user.

properties.name: extra-menu
properties.doc: A menu model whose contents will be appended to the context menu.

properties.name: im-module
properties.doc:
Which IM (input method) module should be used for this text_view.

See [class@Gtk.IMMulticontext].

Setting this to a non-%NULL value overrides the system-wide IM module
setting.

properties.name: indent
properties.doc: Amount to indent the paragraph, in pixels.

properties.name: input-hints
properties.doc:
Additional hints (beyond [property@Gtk.TextView:input-purpose])
that allow input methods to fine-tune their behaviour.

properties.name: input-purpose
properties.doc: The purpose of this text field.

properties.name: justification
properties.doc: Left, right, or center justification.

properties.name: left-margin
properties.doc:
The default left margin for text in the text view.

Tags in the buffer may override the default.

Note that this property is confusingly named.

properties.name: monospace
properties.doc: Whether text should be displayed in a monospace font.

properties.name: overwrite
properties.doc: Whether entered text overwrites existing contents.

properties.name: pixels-above-lines
properties.doc: Pixels of blank space above paragraphs.

properties.name: pixels-below-lines
properties.doc: Pixels of blank space below paragraphs.

properties.name: pixels-inside-wrap
properties.doc: Pixels of blank space between wrapped lines in a paragraph.

properties.name: right-margin
properties.doc:
The default right margin for text in the text view.

Tags in the buffer may override the default.

Note that this property is confusingly named.

properties.name: tabs
properties.doc: Custom tabs for this text.

properties.name: top-margin
properties.doc:
The top margin for text in the text view.

Note that this property is confusingly named.

properties.name: wrap-mode
properties.doc: Whether to wrap lines never, at word boundaries, or at character boundaries.

properties.name: can-focus
properties.doc:
Whether the widget or any of its descendents can accept
the input focus.

properties.name: can-target
properties.doc: Whether the widget can receive pointer events.

properties.name: css-classes
properties.doc: A list of css classes applied to this widget.

properties.name: css-name
properties.doc: The name of this widget in the CSS tree.

properties.name: cursor
properties.doc: The cursor used by @widget.

properties.name: focus-on-click
properties.doc: Whether the widget should grab focus when it is clicked with the mouse.

properties.name: focusable
properties.doc: Whether this widget itself will accept the input focus.

properties.name: halign
properties.doc: How to distribute horizontal space if widget gets extra space.

properties.name: has-default
properties.doc: Whether the widget is the default widget.

properties.name: has-focus
properties.doc: Whether the widget has the input focus.

properties.name: has-tooltip
properties.doc:
Enables or disables the emission of the [signal@Gtk.Widget::query-tooltip]
signal on @widget.

properties.name: height-request
properties.doc: Overrides for height request of the widget.

properties.name: hexpand
properties.doc: Whether to expand horizontally.

properties.name: hexpand-set
properties.doc: Whether to use the `hexpand` property.

properties.name: layout-manager
properties.doc:
The [class@Gtk.LayoutManager] instance to use to compute
the preferred size of the widget, and allocate its children.

properties.name: limit-events
properties.doc:
Makes this widget act like a modal dialog, with respect to
event delivery.

Global event controllers will not handle events with targets
inside the widget, unless they are set up to ignore propagation
limits.

properties.name: margin-bottom
properties.doc: Margin on bottom side of widget.

properties.name: margin-end
properties.doc: Margin on end of widget, horizontally.

properties.name: margin-start
properties.doc: Margin on start of widget, horizontally.

properties.name: margin-top
properties.doc: Margin on top side of widget.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the widget.

properties.name: opacity
properties.doc: The requested opacity of the widget.

properties.name: overflow
properties.doc: How content outside the widget's content area is treated.

properties.name: parent
properties.doc: The parent widget of this widget.

properties.name: receives-default
properties.doc: Whether the widget will receive the default action when it is focused.

properties.name: root
properties.doc: The `GtkRoot` widget of the widget tree containing this widget.

properties.name: scale-factor
properties.doc: The scale factor of the widget.

properties.name: sensitive
properties.doc: Whether the widget responds to input.

properties.name: tooltip-markup
properties.doc:
Sets the text of tooltip to be the given string, which is marked up
with Pango markup.

properties.name: tooltip-text
properties.doc: Sets the text of tooltip to be the given string.

properties.name: valign
properties.doc: How to distribute vertical space if widget gets extra space.

properties.name: vexpand
properties.doc: Whether to expand vertically.

properties.name: vexpand-set
properties.doc: Whether to use the `vexpand` property.

properties.name: visible
properties.doc: Whether the widget is visible.

properties.name: width-request
properties.doc: Overrides for width request of the widget.

signals.name: backspace
signals.doc: Gets emitted when the user asks for it.

signals.name: copy-clipboard
signals.doc: Gets emitted to copy the selection to the clipboard.

signals.name: cut-clipboard
signals.doc: Gets emitted to cut the selection to the clipboard.

signals.name: delete-from-cursor
signals.doc: Gets emitted when the user initiates a text deletion.

signals.name: extend-selection
signals.doc: Emitted when the selection needs to be extended at @location.

signals.name: insert-at-cursor
signals.doc:
Gets emitted when the user initiates the insertion of a
fixed string at the cursor.

signals.name: insert-emoji
signals.doc: Gets emitted to present the Emoji chooser for the @text_view.

signals.name: move-cursor
signals.doc:
Gets emitted when the user initiates a cursor movement.

The ::move-cursor signal is a [keybinding signal](class.SignalAction.html).
If the cursor is not visible in @text_view, this signal causes
the viewport to be moved instead.

Applications should not connect to it, but may emit it with
g_signal_emit_by_name() if they need to control the cursor
programmatically.


The default bindings for this signal come in two variants,
the variant with the <kbd>Shift</kbd> modifier extends the
selection, the variant without it does not.
There are too many key combinations to list them all here.

- <kbd>←</kbd>, <kbd>→</kbd>, <kbd>↑</kbd>, <kbd>↓</kbd>
  move by individual characters/lines
- <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>←</kbd>, etc.

signals.name: move-viewport
signals.doc:
Gets emitted to move the viewport.

The ::move-viewport signal is a [keybinding signal](class.SignalAction.html),
which can be bound to key combinations to allow the user to move the viewport,
i.e.

signals.name: paste-clipboard
signals.doc:
Gets emitted to paste the contents of the clipboard
into the text view.

signals.name: preedit-changed
signals.doc:
Emitted when preedit text of the active IM changes.

If an input method is used, the typed text will not immediately
be committed to the buffer.

signals.name: select-all
signals.doc: Gets emitted to select or unselect the complete contents of the text view.

signals.name: set-anchor
signals.doc:
Gets emitted when the user initiates settings the "anchor" mark.

The ::set-anchor signal is a [keybinding signal](class.SignalAction.html)
which gets emitted when the user initiates setting the "anchor"
mark.

signals.name: toggle-cursor-visible
signals.doc: Gets emitted to toggle the `cursor-visible` property.

signals.name: toggle-overwrite
signals.doc: Gets emitted to toggle the overwrite mode of the text view.

signals.name: destroy
signals.doc:
Signals that all holders of a reference to the widget should release
the reference that they hold.

signals.name: direction-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the text direction of a widget changes.

signals.name: hide
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is hidden.

signals.name: keynav-failed
signals.doc: Emitted if keyboard navigation fails.

signals.name: map
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is going to be mapped.

signals.name: mnemonic-activate
signals.doc: Emitted when a widget is activated via a mnemonic.

signals.name: move-focus
signals.doc: Emitted when the focus is moved.

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine

name: GtkTooltip
doc:
Represents a widget tooltip.

Basic tooltips can be realized simply by using
[method@Gtk.Widget.set_tooltip_text] or
[method@Gtk.Widget.set_tooltip_markup] without
any explicit tooltip object.

When you need a tooltip with a little more fancy contents,
like adding an image, or you want the tooltip to have different
contents per `GtkTreeView` row or cell, you will have to do a
little more work:

- Set the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property to %TRUE.
  This will make GTK monitor the widget for motion and related events
  which are needed to determine when and where to show a tooltip.

- Connect to the [signal@Gtk.Widget::query-tooltip] signal.
  This signal will be emitted when a tooltip is supposed to be shown.
  One of the arguments passed to the signal handler is a `GtkTooltip`
  object.

If %FALSE, the `GListModel` functions for this object return custom

doc: The type of item used by `GtkTreeListModel`.

properties.doc: The model holding the row's children.

doc: Applies a gives sorter to the levels in a tree.

properties.name: sorter
properties.doc: The underlying sorter

signals.name: changed
signals.doc:
Emitted whenever the sorter changed.

Users of the sorter should then update the sort order
again via gtk_sorter_compare().

[class@Gtk.SortListModel] handles this signal automatically.

Depending on the @change parameter, it may be possible to update
the sort order without a full resorting.

name: GtkTreeModelFilter
doc:
A `GtkTreeModel` which hides parts of an underlying tree model

A `GtkTreeModelFilter` is a tree model which wraps another tree model,
and can do the following things:

- Filter specific rows, based on data from a “visible column”, a column
  storing booleans indicating whether the row should be filtered or not,
  or based on the return value of a “visible function”, which gets a
  model, iter and user_data and returns a boolean indicating whether the
  row should be filtered or not.

- Modify the “appearance” of the model, using a modify function.
  This is extremely powerful and allows for just changing some
  values and also for creating a completely different model based
  on the given child model.

- Set a different root node, also known as a “virtual root”.

properties.name: child-model
properties.doc: The child model of the tree model filter.

properties.name: virtual-root
properties.doc: The virtual root of the tree model filter.

name: GtkTreeModelSort
doc:
A GtkTreeModel which makes an underlying tree model sortable

The `GtkTreeModelSort` is a model which implements the `GtkTreeSortable`
interface.

properties.name: model
properties.doc: The model of the tree model sort.

name: GtkTreeStore

The `GtkTreeStore` object is a list model for use with a `GtkTreeView`
widget.

name: GtkTreeView
doc: A widget for displaying both trees and lists Widget that displays any object that implements the [iface@Gtk.TreeModel] interface. Please refer to the [tree widget conceptual overview](section-tree-widget.html) for an overview of all the objects and data types related to the tree widget and how they work together. ## Coordinate systems in GtkTreeView API Several different coordinate systems are exposed in the `GtkTreeView` API. These are: ![](tree-view-coordinates.png) - Widget coordinates: Coordinates relative to the widget (usually `widget->window`). - Bin window coordinates: Coordinates relative to the window that GtkTreeView renders to. - Tree coordinates: Coordinates relative to the entire scrollable area of GtkTreeView.

name: GtkWidget
doc:
The base class for all widgets.

It manages the widget lifecycle, layout, states and style.

### Height-for-width Geometry Management

GTK uses a height-for-width (and width-for-height) geometry management
system.

name: GtkWidgetPaintable
doc:
A `GdkPaintable` that displays the contents of a widget.

`GtkWidgetPaintable` will also take care of the widget not being in a
state where it can be drawn (like when it isn't shown) and just draw
nothing or where it does not have a size (like when it is hidden) and
report no size in that case.

Of course, `GtkWidgetPaintable` allows you to monitor widgets for size
changes by emitting the [signal@Gdk.Paintable::invalidate-size] signal
whenever the size of the widget changes as well as for visual changes by
emitting the [signal@Gdk.Paintable::invalidate-contents] signal whenever
the widget changes.

You can use a `GtkWidgetPaintable` everywhere a `GdkPaintable` is allowed,
including using it on a `GtkPicture` (or one of its parents) that it was
set on itself via gtk_picture_set_paintable().

properties.name: widget
properties.doc: The observed widget or %NULL if none.

name: GtkWindow
doc: A toplevel window which can contain other widgets. Windows normally have decorations that are under the control of the windowing system and allow the user to manipulate the window (resize it, move it, close it,...). # GtkWindow as GtkBuildable The `GtkWindow` implementation of the [iface@Gtk.Buildable] interface supports setting a child as the titlebar by specifying “titlebar” as the “type” attribute of a `` element. # Shortcuts and Gestures `GtkWindow` supports the following keyboard shortcuts: - F10 activates the menubar, if present. - Alt makes the mnemonics visible while pressed. The following signals have default keybindings: - [signal@Gtk.Window::activate-default] - [signal@Gtk.Window::activate-focus] - [signal@Gtk.Window::enable-debugging] # Actions `GtkWindow` defines a set of built-in actions: - `default.activate` activates the default widget. - `window.minimize` minimizes the window. - `window.toggle-maximized` maximizes or restores the window. - `window.close` closes the window. # CSS nodes ``` window.background [.csd / .solid-csd / .ssd] [.maximized / .fullscreen / .tiled] ├── ╰── .titlebar [.default-decoration] ``` `GtkWindow` has a main CSS node with name window and style class .background. Style classes that are typically used with the main CSS node are .csd (when client-side decorations are in use), .solid-csd (for client-side decorations without invisible borders), .ssd (used by mutter when rendering server-side decorations).

name: GtkWindowControls
doc: Shows window frame controls. Typical window frame controls are minimize, maximize and close buttons, and the window icon. `GtkWindowControls` only displays start or end side of the controls (see [property@Gtk.WindowControls:side]), so it's intended to be always used in pair with another `GtkWindowControls` for the opposite side, for example: ```xml ``` # CSS nodes ``` windowcontrols ├── [image.icon] ├── [button.minimize] ├── [button.maximize] ╰── [button.close] ``` A `GtkWindowControls`' CSS node is called windowcontrols.

properties.name: decoration-layout

signals.doc:
Signals that all holders of a reference to the widget should release

signals.name: keynav-failed
signals.doc: Emitted if keyboard navigation fails.

signals.name: map
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is going to be mapped.

signals.name: mnemonic-activate
signals.doc: Emitted when a widget is activated via a mnemonic.

signals.name: move-focus
signals.doc: Emitted when the focus is moved.

signals.name: query-tooltip
signals.doc:
Emitted when the widget’s tooltip is about to be shown.

This happens when the [property@Gtk.Widget:has-tooltip] property
is true and the hover timeout has expired with the cursor hovering
above @widget; or emitted when @widget got focus in keyboard mode.

Using the given coordinates, the signal handler should determine
whether a tooltip should be shown for @widget.

signals.name: realize
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is associated with a `GdkSurface`.

signals.name: show
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is shown.

signals.name: state-flags-changed
signals.doc: Emitted when the widget state changes.

signals.name: unmap
signals.doc: Emitted when @widget is going to be unmapped.

signals.name: unrealize
signals.doc: Emitted when the `GdkSurface` associated with @widget is destroyed.

name: GtkWindowGroup
doc:
Creates groups of windows that behave like separate applications.

It achieves this by limiting the effect of GTK grabs and modality
to windows in the same group.

A window can be a member in at most one window group at a time.
Windows that have not been explicitly assigned to a group are
implicitly treated like windows of the default window group.

`GtkWindowGroup` objects are referenced by each window in the group,
so once you have added all windows to a `GtkWindowGroup`, you can drop
the initial reference to the window group with g_object_unref().

name: GtkWindowHandle
doc: Implements titlebar functionality for a window.
